findings.title: Wallet login accepts ANY signature (verifyWalletSignature is a placeholder returning true)
findings.impact: POST /api/auth/wallet/verify -> authenticateWallet -> verifyWalletSignature always returns true (TODO placeholder). Any caller can POST a valid-shaped address + arbitrary 0x-hex signature and receive a 30-day session for that wallet address, i.e. full account takeover of any wallet-auth user without possessing the key. Zod (AuthWalletVerifySchema) only enforces string shape, not cryptography. Launch-unsafe if wallet auth is exposed.
findings.fix: Implement real EIP-191/EIP-1271 signature recovery (ethers verifyMessage) and compare recovered address to the claimed address before minting a session; fail closed. Until then, disable the wallet auth route in production.

findings.title: Unauthenticated, unvalidated, un-budgeted Opus endpoint — anonymous cost DoS
findings.impact: Any unauthenticated internet user can POST arbitrary JSON and trigger a claude-opus-4-8 (MODELS.premium) call with 1500 max_tokens. No session check (getUserFromSession not even imported), no zod, no checkBudget/tier gate, no rate limit (middleware.ts is headers-only). This exceeds the calibrated paid+ungated=HIGH case: it stacks missing auth AND the most expensive model. A trivial loop burns real money at Opus rates on launch day.
findings.fix: Require session (mirror mindmap/re-extract route.ts:19-21), add a zod schema in route-schemas.ts (topicName/relatedTopics/lastQueries with length caps — lastQueries is interpolated into the prompt unbounded at :88), run checkBudget (lib/token-budget.ts) before the call, and consider MODELS.budget instead of premium for a suggestions feature.

findings.title: Unauthenticated SSRF with response reflection (GET raw ?url=)
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: Unauthenticated SSRF, partial reflection + 302 redirect pivot
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: Real Anthropic + OpenRouter API keys in pushed git history; rotation unverified
findings.impact: Three real keys are recoverable from history: (1) sk-ant-api03-yRj… (108 chars, real key length) at SESSION_RESUME_MAR17_2026.md:93-94 in commit a050fe3 (2026-03-18), removed in 5cb5549 — BOTH commits are ancestors of current origin/main, so the key is fetchable from GitHub right now; (2) sk-or-v1-c5d14c3… (73 chars, real shape) at RESUME.md:25 in commits 6861c01/eee0318, also on origin/main history; (3) sk-ant-api03-AF6… (108 chars) at .specstory/history/2025-12-14_15-35Z-akhai-full-diagnostic-and-audit.md:58766 in commit 7473d58, now only in local refs/original/* filter-branch backups but present in the pre-rewrite main that was previously pushed (GitHub retains dangling objects fetchable by hash). Commit a58281b (2026-04-17, 'security: redact all leaked API keys') explicitly notes 'Keys exist in git history — rotate on dashboards' — three months later the repo gives no evidence rotation happened. If still active: anyone who clones/fetches the repo can drain the Anthropic and OpenRouter accounts and impersonate the service.
findings.fix: Before freeze: confirm on the Anthropic and OpenRouter dashboards that these three keys are revoked; if not, rotate immediately (rotation is the real fix — history scrubbing is secondary). Then optionally git-filter-repo the two SESSION/RESUME commits + force-push, ask GitHub support to GC dangling objects, and delete local refs/original/* + expire reflogs. Add a gitleaks/pre-commit secret scan — this is the third distinct file type (specstory transcript, RESUME.md, SESSION_RESUME) to capture live keys, so the pattern will recur without tooling. Downgrade to LOW once rotation is confirmed.

findings.title: Wallet signature verification is a no-op stub that returns true — full wallet auth bypass
findings.impact:
verifyWalletSignature() ignores address/signature/message and unconditionally `return true` (lib/auth.ts:129). authenticateWallet() (lib/auth.ts:135-157) therefore mints a valid 30-day session for whatever address the caller supplies. The route (app/api/auth/wallet/verify/route.ts) only checks that the message string matches the `Address: <addr>
Timestamp: <ts>` template and that the signature is 0x-hex shaped (route-schemas.ts:20-29) — no cryptographic binding. An attacker POSTs {address: <any victim wallet>, signature:'0x00', message:'Sign this message to authenticate with AkhAI.

Address:<victim>
Timestamp:<any>'} and is authenticated as that wallet's user (createOrGetUser('wallet', address.toLowerCase())). Complete impersonation of any wallet account, including one previously linked to a GitHub user via linked_accounts. Launch-unsafe.
findings.fix: Implement real EIP-191/EIP-1271 verification (e.g. viem verifyMessage / ethers recoverAddress) before creating the session; reject when the recovered address !== supplied address. Until then, wallet login must be disabled, not shipped.

findings.title: Standalone deploy cannot bootstrap a fresh host — hard dependency on legacy tree that won't exist after the Aug-8 migration
findings.impact: Lines 25/28/29 copy .env.local from /home/akhai/app, symlink data/ into it, and harvest the linux better-sqlite3 binding from its node_modules/.pnpm. On a fresh box (FlokiNET is down per TIMELINE.md:27,40 — Hetzner switch likely) /home/akhai/app is empty: `cp` aborts the script under set -e, the sqlite binding is 'NOT FOUND', and setup-vps.sh provisions only the legacy pnpm-build flow (Node 20, build-essential). There is currently no working path from repo to a new production host.
findings.fix: Add a fresh-host bootstrap to the standalone flow: upload a filled .env.production, create the data dir directly (no legacy symlink), and produce the linux better-sqlite3 binding deterministically (npm rebuild in a container matching the VPS Node version, or a pinned prebuilt binary). Remove all reads of /home/akhai/app.

findings.title: Stripe token-credit purchases grant zero tokens (key mismatch)
findings.impact: Pricing page sells credits with planIds 'starter'/'builder'/'scale'/'bulk' (pricing-config.ts:78-104; PricingCard.tsx:67 sends planId:id; checkout route.ts:63-66 stores only planId in metadata), but the webhook resolves tokens via TOKEN_CREDIT_TIERS keyed 'credit-10'/'credit-50'/'credit-100'/'credit-500' (subscription.ts:41-46). TOKEN_CREDIT_TIERS['starter'] is undefined -> tokens=0 -> the `if (tokens > 0)` grant is skipped. Every card credit purchase charges the user and delivers nothing — the maximal honesty failure for a product whose brand is honesty. The two tables also disagree on token amounts ($100 = 3M on the pricing page vs 2M in subscription.ts).
findings.fix: Single-source the credit tiers: key TOKEN_CREDIT_TIERS by the TOKEN_CREDITS ids (or pass tokens through checkout metadata and read it in the webhook), and add a webhook test that a 'starter' purchase grants exactly 100,000 tokens.

findings.title: Paid subscriptions never change the enforced tier — Pro buyers keep free-tier 50K/day
findings.impact: Budget enforcement reads users.tier ('free'|'pro'|'legend', db/auth.ts:15) and coerces anything else to 'free' (token-budget.ts:28-31). No code path updates users.tier: the Stripe webhook writes only to the subscriptions table, and grep finds no `UPDATE users ... tier` anywhere except the generic updateUser helper (db/auth.ts:70) which no purchase flow calls. A $20/mo Pro user promised 'Unlimited queries' (pricing-config.ts:34) is budget-capped at the free 50,000 tokens/day; 'instinct' and 'team' are not even representable in the users.tier enum. Also note QUERY_LIMITS.free=3 (subscription.ts:50) contradicts the advertised '10 queries per day' and is dead code.
findings.fix: On checkout.session.completed, update users.tier from planId (and unify the three tier taxonomies: token-budget 'free|pro|legend', subscription 'free|pro|instinct|team', pricing page). Reconcile the Pro promise ('Unlimited queries') with the actual 1M token/day budget or change the copy to state the budget.

findings.title: UI claims '92% savings' for cod but the shipped implementation is a token-multiplying self-refine, not Chain of Draft
findings.impact: False user-facing economics claim at freeze gate. The CoD paper's 92% saving comes from <=5-word draft steps capped at ~300 output tokens (implemented in core cod.ts:85-90 but never called by web — only core/index.ts:90 and registry.ts:59 reference executeChainOfDraft). The web prompt (query-pipeline.ts:308) instead demands [DRAFT 1]+[REFLECTION]+[DRAFT 2]+[FINAL ANSWER], i.e. the answer written ~3x in output tokens (the ~4-5x-priced token class per output-budgets.ts:1), making 'cod' the most output-expensive single-call methodology while being sold as the cheapest. Registry metadata doubles down: tokenMultiplier 0.08, '92% cheaper' (core registry.ts:59-72).
findings.fix: Either wire the real executeChainOfDraft for cod, or rename/re-describe the web behavior (METHOD_PLAIN 'Draft and refine' at thought-stream.ts:160 is already honest) and delete the 92%/token/cost claims in methodology-data.ts:58, MethodologyExplorer.tsx:84ff, MethodologyFrame.tsx:51-57, registry.ts:59-72.

findings.title: Public claim mismatch: UI says 'Program of Thought — runs code in a sandbox'; runtime is prompt-only Plan-and-Solve
findings.impact: Users are told pas 'generates executable code, runs it' and 'Run the code in a sandbox' (MethodologyExplorer.tsx:178-186, dashboard/page.tsx:84 'Generate & run code', IntelligenceBadge.tsx:41, MethodologyFrame.tsx:81, ReportConsoleDrawer.tsx:292, philosophy:40, help:118 'PoT'). What actually executes is getMethodologyPrompt('pas') — a Plan-and-Solve prompt block, Wang et al. ACL 2023 (query-pipeline.ts:317) with no code generation or execution; route.ts:798-844 sends it through the generic callProvider branch. Math answers come from LLM arithmetic — the exact failure mode PoT exists to fix — and 'pas' is cacheable (query-cache.ts:41), so a wrong arithmetic answer gets cached and re-served.
findings.fix: Pick one identity before freeze: either rename all UI copy to Plan-and-Solve (methodology-data.ts:258 already says this) and drop the code-execution claims, or actually wire the core PoT executor (after fixing its sandbox). Copy-only fix is ~6 files.

findings.title: Core PoT 'sandbox' is not a sandbox and its timeout cannot fire (currently dead code, but registry advertises it as implemented)
findings.impact: executeJavaScript uses new Function('console','Math', code) (pas.ts:498) — only console and Math are shadowed; LLM-generated code retains full access to globalThis, process.env (API keys), and fetch → env exfiltration if ever wired to user input. The 5000ms timeout (pas.ts:485-487) is a setTimeout racing a synchronous fn() call (pas.ts:501): an infinite loop blocks the event loop so the timer never fires — executionTimeout is unenforceable, a while(true) hangs the server process. registry.ts:119 claims 'sandboxed Function constructor and timeout protection'. Unreachable today (see dead-code finding), so not a live blocker — but the natural fix for the claim-mismatch finding is wiring this, which would make it one.
findings.fix: Do not wire as-is. If PoT is wanted, execute in an isolated worker/child process with hard kill timeout and no env inheritance (e.g. worker_threads + resourceLimits, or a vm2/isolated-vm class solution). Also correct registry.ts:119 and pas.ts:462 comments now so nobody trusts the 'sandbox' label.

findings.title: React agent path drops conversation history, side-canal context, page context, and system prompt
findings.impact: runReactAgent(query, undefined, cb) receives ONLY the raw query. The messages array built at route.ts:459-474 (last-6 history + sideCanalContext), the ~9KB methodology systemPrompt (route.ts:481), and dynamicContext incl. pageContext (route.ts:539) are all unused on this branch. A follow-up like 'what about its price?' routed to react loses every referent and answers about nothing; answers also skip house style, ## SYNTHESIS, [RELATED]/[NEXT] — sections downstream parsing/rendering expects.
findings.fix: Thread conversation history into runReactAgent's prompt (AI SDK generateText accepts messages[]) and pass the base-identity system text; or at minimum prepend last-N turns + pageContext to the prompt string.

findings.title: User-facing 'sc' claims are false in default prod: 'Multiple drafts' / 'Self-Consistency voting' while one draft runs, no voting exists
findings.impact: METHOD_PLAIN sc:'Multiple drafts' (thought-stream.ts:165) is streamed live to users as 'Thinking method: Multiple drafts' (query-fusion.ts:217,250-251), and methodology-data.ts:64 tooltips 'Self-Consistency voting' — but with SC_MULTIPATH unset (default, route.ts:700-703) sc executes exactly ONE callProvider pass (route.ts:798-817). No sampling, no drafts, no voting. Even flag-on, selection is centrality-pick, not voting. Three conflicting public names for one id: 'Multiple drafts', 'Self-Consistency' (methodology-data.ts:195), 'Buffer of Thoughts' (IntelligenceBadge.tsx:39, dashboard/page.tsx:82, ReportConsoleDrawer.tsx:287). For a product pitched on methodology transparency this is a truth-in-labeling failure on the primary live-status surface.
findings.fix: Pick one name and one honest description. Default-off: METHOD_PLAIN sc → 'Buffered-fact validation' (matches actual single-pass behavior); drop 'voting' from methodology-data.ts:64. If/when SC_MULTIPATH ships on, switch the live string conditionally.

findings.title: Empty advisor content treated as status 'complete' — can defeat the anti-fake-consensus gate
findings.impact: callProvider returns status:'complete' when data.choices is missing/empty (content:'' at line 135, status 'complete' at 151) even though line 137 explicitly records the 'empty' outcome to COGS. Empty advisors count toward successfulRound1 (line 269), so 3 empty replies bypass the all-failed 502 honesty gate (lines 271-281) and Mother Base is asked to 'synthesize' from headers-only context — a fabricated consensus, the exact failure the 502 gate exists to prevent. Empties also enter round-2 context and consensus scoring.
findings.fix: In callProvider, return status:'failed' (or a distinct 'empty') when content is falsy, mirroring the existing recordAdvisorCogs 'empty' outcome; downstream filters on status==='complete' then behave correctly.

findings.title: Single-model fallback answers stay labeled methodology 'tot' → UI shows 'Multi-AI consensus'
findings.impact: Anonymous users (the `&& userId` gate at 294; auto can route them to tot via query-pipeline.ts:230-243 with no user gate) and any consensus failure (catch at 365-372 falls through to standard processing) get ONE model's answer, but buildResponseData returns methodology/methodologyUsed = selectedMethod.id = 'tot' (query-response-builder.ts:145-146) and the live panel renders METHOD_PLAIN['tot'] = 'Multi-AI consensus' (thought-stream.ts:164). For a product whose core claim is consensus, mislabeled single-model answers contradict the tot-consensus route's own stated invariant ('honest single-model fallback', route.ts:273-274).
findings.fix: On the anonymous skip and the gtpError fall-through, rewrite selectedMethod to the actually-executed methodology (e.g. {id:'direct', reason:'tot unavailable — single-model fallback'}) before the standard path builds events/response.

findings.title: conversationHistory accepted, validated, then silently dropped — tot follow-ups are context-blind
findings.impact: TotConsensusSchema validates conversationHistory (route-schemas.ts:89-91), simple-query passes it (simple-query/route.ts:315), it is destructured at line 188 and never referenced again: round 1 (line 240 area), round 2 (line 325), and synthesis (synthesis-stream.ts:45) all use only the raw query. Any follow-up in a tot conversation ('expand on point 2') gets a consensus over zero context — visibly wrong answers on every multi-turn tot use.
findings.fix: Inject a bounded slice of conversationHistory into the round-1 user prompt and buildSynthesisPrompt (e.g. last N turns, truncated), or reject/annotate tot for follow-ups; either way stop accepting a field that is ignored.

findings.title: tot/GTP consensus path drops all layer weights — flagship path is layer-inert
findings.impact: For any signed-in query resolved to 'tot' (the 4-AI consensus, the product's premium flagship), the proxy body is JSON.stringify({ query, conversationHistory, queryId }) — no layersWeights, no fusion enhancement, no dynamicContext. The branch returns at line 356 BEFORE the prompt build (line 456+), so generateEnhancedSystemPrompt is never applied. Every slider in the Layer Console provably changes NOTHING in any provider call on this path (weights only influenced auto-routing INTO tot). No file in /api/tot-consensus reads layersWeights (grep confirms zero hits).
findings.fix: Pass layersWeights (or the generated fusion enhancement string) in the tot-consensus body and inject it into the consensus/synthesis system prompts; or visibly disable the layer console for tot and say so in the UI.

findings.title: Round-2 skip threshold (0.85) is mathematically unreachable — round 2 always runs
findings.impact: calculateConsensus caps at 1 - 1/n overlap: each key point is deduped per advisor (consensus-scoring.ts:25), so a string appears at most once per advisor, giving max overlap 2/3 with 3 advisors and max consensus 0.5 + 0.333 = 0.833 < 0.85. The 'if needed' cross-pollination gate is dead code: every full round 1 triggers round 2, adding 3 advisor calls (route doc at lines 42-43 says free tier is ~20 req/min shared) and up to 35s latency (35000ms budget, line 326) to every GTP query, for zero adaptive benefit.
findings.fix: Either lower the gate below the reachable ceiling (e.g. 0.65) with a comment deriving the max, or replace the exact-string overlap metric first (see next finding) and re-derive the threshold. Alternatively make round 2 explicit-always and delete the illusory gate.

findings.title: Quality asserts are prompt-echo keywords — a question-restating non-answer passes 9/13
findings.impact: icontains targets ('handshake', 'graphql', 'node', 'ai', 'monolith', 'remote', 'sqlite', 'gdpr', 'wal') all appear verbatim in the prompts. An engine that degrades to restating the question passes the quality bar at 9/13 = 69%... combined with the two math regexes only 2 tests measure actual correctness. The 85-threshold gate measures fluency, not answers.
findings.fix: Replace keyword asserts with facts NOT present in the prompt (e.g. 'client hello'/'certificate' for TLS, a version number regex for Node LTS) or llm-rubric asserts for the open-ended ones.

findings.title: Methodology routing never asserted — the router-flip gate cannot detect a router regression
findings.impact: README.md:9 says this gate must pass 'before router flip (Block 5)', and the provider surfaces metadata.methodology (providers/akhai-sse.js:44), but no test asserts it. A regression routing every query to Direct (skipping PoT math, GTP consensus, guard-heavy paths) passes all 15 goldens. The comment 'span all 7 methodologies' (queries.yaml:1) is aspirational, not verified.
findings.fix: Add javascript asserts on context/metadata.methodology per golden (Direct/PoT/GTP at minimum), and add forced-methodology goldens (provider currently hardcodes methodology:'auto' at akhai-sse.js:15).

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated route calls paid Anthropic API with no checkBudget/tier gate
findings.impact: POST /api/arboreal-chat resolves to an anonymous user (never rejects — resolveUserId:29-33) and calls callProvider('anthropic', {model: MODELS.mid, maxTokens:1024}) at line 62, which hits api.anthropic.com (paid). Unlike simple-query (which runs checkBudget(user) at route.ts:273 before every paid call), this handler has NO budget or tier gate anywhere. Any unauthenticated caller can loop POSTs to run unlimited paid Claude inference — direct, uncapped cost/DoS on the Anthropic bill.
findings.fix: Insert checkBudget(user) (from lib/token-budget) before the callProvider call and reject with 429 when !allowed, mirroring simple-query/route.ts:273. Consider requiring a real (non-anonymous) session, or route anonymous callers to the free model as simple-query does.

findings.title: Unauthenticated, unmetered paid Anthropic calls (budget bypass)
findings.impact: Handler reads the body with no auth check and no checkBudget/tier gate, then calls callAnthropic (Haiku->Sonnet, max_tokens 4096, up to 2 model attempts) via extractCognitiveSignature/restructureThinkingIntoLenses. checkBudget exists only in simple-query (lib/token-budget.ts:71, used at app/api/simple-query). Anonymous callers can drive uncapped Anthropic spend. Body is also cast to an interface with no zod validation.
findings.fix: Require a valid session (getUserFromSession on session_token, 401 otherwise) and run checkBudget before the LLM call; add a real zod schema for the POST body instead of the SignatureRequest cast.

findings.title: Unauthenticated, unmetered paid Anthropic calls (budget bypass)
findings.impact: No session check and no budget gate before generateSynthesis -> callAnthropic (Haiku->Sonnet, max_tokens 4096). exchanges[] is caller-controlled and unbounded, so prompt size (and cost) scales with attacker input. Body cast to SynthesisRequest with no zod. Anonymous uncapped Anthropic spend.
findings.fix: Gate behind a session + checkBudget; bound exchanges[] length and per-exchange text; validate the body with a zod schema.

findings.title: Unauthenticated, unmetered paid Anthropic call (budget bypass)
findings.impact: callProvider('anthropic', ...) (MODELS.budget/Haiku, maxTokens 1500) with openrouter-free fallback runs with no session check and no checkBudget/tier gate anywhere in the handler. query accepts up to 10000 chars. Anonymous callers can drive repeated paid Anthropic spend; body IS zod-validated (CanvasVizSchema) so this is purely the auth/budget gap.
findings.fix: Require a valid session and run checkBudget before callProvider; consider rate-limiting per user/IP.

findings.title: unvalidated_routes ratchet is evadable — 3 unvalidated POST bodies slip through
findings.impact: The A2 ratchet greps files matching literal 'request.json()' and passes any file that merely contains '.parse('/'Schema'/'parsed.'. (1) btcpay-checkout uses req.json() (route.ts:34) so it is never counted at all; (2) cognitive-signature and (3) conversation-synthesis contain an incidental JSON.parse(row...) on cached DB rows (route.ts:47 each), which satisfies the grep while their actual request bodies are cast to interfaces with zero validation. So the '0 unvalidated routes' claim is false for these three POST handlers.
findings.fix: Harden the ratchet to detect body-var-agnostic '.json()' calls and require the parsed body (not any '.parse(') to flow through a zod schema; then add zod schemas to btcpay-checkout, cognitive-signature, and conversation-synthesis.

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated route calls Opus (MODELS.premium) with no checkBudget
findings.impact: Any anonymous client can loop POSTs with 10K-char queries into claude-opus-4-8; checkBudget/tier gating exists only in simple-query/route.ts:277, so this is unmetered Anthropic spend with zero auth.
findings.fix: Require session (getUserFromSession + 401) and run checkBudget(user) before callProvider, mirroring simple-query; or downgrade to MODELS.budget with a per-IP rate limit at minimum.

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated Opus call with up to ~800KB client-controlled prompt history
findings.impact: NatalChatSchema allows 100 messages x 8000 chars each, all interpolated into an Opus (premium) call with no auth and no budget gate — worst input-token abuse surface in this chunk.
findings.fix: Add session check + checkBudget before callProvider; tighten ChatMessagesSchema total-size bound (e.g. cumulative 20K chars).

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated Opus call, no checkBudget
findings.impact: Same pattern: anonymous POST -> callProvider('anthropic', MODELS.premium, 600 tokens). Unmetered paid spend; .passthrough() chart objects let arbitrary extra JSON ride into the prompt payload.
findings.fix: Session check + checkBudget before the paid call; consider stripping passthrough keys to a size-bounded whitelist.

findings.title: Budget bypass + no auth + no zod: direct anthropic.messages.create on every anonymous POST
findings.impact: Unauthenticated, schema-less route (raw destructure at line 327) burns a Haiku call (800 max_tokens) per request plus Brave Search paid quota fallback; trivially scriptable spend drain.
findings.fix: Add EnhancedLinksSchema (query/conversationContext/topics with max lengths), session check, and checkBudget before extractSearchQueries.

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated, unvalidated POST triggers Haiku->Sonnet chain (max_tokens 4096)
findings.impact: Anonymous callers submit 12K-char bodies that hit api.anthropic.com (lib/depth/llm-extractor.ts:40) with a Sonnet retry on failure — two paid calls per request possible, no auth, no budget gate, body cast via TS interface only (line 44).
findings.fix: Add zod schema (queryId/response/query with max lengths), require session, and gate on checkBudget; verify queryId exists and belongs to the caller before extracting.

findings.title: Cross-user annotation cache poisoning via client-supplied queryId (unauthenticated write)
findings.impact: UPDATE queries SET annotations WHERE id = ? runs with no ownership check: anyone who knows/guesses a query id can overwrite its cached annotations with attacker-influenced LLM output, which the cache path (lines 52-63) then serves to the row's real owner — stored content injection into another user's UI.
findings.fix: Scope the UPDATE and the cache SELECT by user_id (WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?) after adding auth, matching the export/[id] ownership pattern.

findings.title: Unauthenticated /api/debug leaks internal logs (user content fragments, userIds) and allows log clearing
findings.impact: GET returns the last 100 log entries (lib/logger.ts:189-199) which include user side-canal context substrings (simple-query/route.ts:453) and userIds (crypto-checkout/route.ts:41) plus API-key presence booleans — cross-user info disclosure to any anonymous visitor in production. DELETE (line 28) lets anyone wipe the in-memory log buffer.
findings.fix: Gate both handlers behind an admin session check (or return 404 when NODE_ENV === 'production'); scrub user data from log payloads regardless.

findings.title: Unauthenticated endpoint fires 5 paid Sonnet calls per request, no budget gate
findings.impact: callAgent routes every council agent to anthropic MODELS.mid when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set (lines 24-25); one anonymous POST = 5 paid calls (4 perspectives line 61 + synthesizer line 83). checkBudget exists only in simple-query — a curl loop drains the Anthropic account with 5x amplification. Side effect: the 'multi-AI' council is silently single-provider in prod.
findings.fix: Require a session and run checkBudget/tier gating before the fan-out, as simple-query does (~line 270); add per-IP rate limiting for anonymous access if it must stay open.

findings.title: Unauthenticated SSE endpoint makes 4 paid Sonnet calls, no budget gate
findings.impact: Entity extraction (line 61, 500 tok) plus 3 parallel branch predictions (lines 92-105, 800 tok each) on hardcoded 'anthropic' with no auth, budget check, or rate limit — ~2900 output tokens of Sonnet per anonymous request.
findings.fix: Gate behind session + checkBudget before starting the stream; return 401/402 pre-stream.

findings.title: Unauthenticated paid Anthropic call (agent-chat), no budget gate
findings.impact: callProvider at line 31 reaches Sonnet (fallback at lines 26-27) for any anonymous caller; combined with scenario-chat (scenario-chat/route.ts:38, same pattern) these are free-to-attacker, paid-to-owner chat endpoints with unbounded request volume.
findings.fix: Same treatment as simple-query: session check + checkBudget before callProvider, in both agent-chat and scenario-chat.

findings.title: Unauthenticated direct api.anthropic.com call, no budget gate, bypasses callProvider
findings.impact: Anonymous POST triggers a paid Haiku call (line 81, MODELS.budget, 300 tok). Cheapest of the ungated routes but still an open cost drain, and the raw fetch bypasses callProvider so central cost/latency instrumentation never sees these calls.
findings.fix: Route through callProvider with session + budget gating, or at minimum add per-IP rate limiting.

findings.title: History list leaks all anonymous/legacy queries+results to every authenticated user
findings.impact: getRecentQueries uses 'WHERE user_id = ? OR user_id IS NULL' (line 72), invoked from app/api/history/route.ts:25. Any signed-up user sees the full query text AND result of every query ever made without a session — cross-user privacy leak that directly contradicts the LOCK policy enforced (and documented) in history/[id]/conversation/route.ts:21.
findings.fix: Drop the 'OR user_id IS NULL' widening to match the conversation route's owner-only policy; if legacy rows must remain reachable, migrate them behind an explicit admin surface.

findings.title: SSRF with response readback — no private-address blocking, GET bypasses zod
findings.impact: GET passes raw ?url= (line 93) to fetchURLContent with zero validation; POST is schema-capped but the fetcher itself has no guard: fetchWebpageContent (lib/url-content-fetcher.ts:185) fetches any http(s) URL — http://localhost:3000, http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data, internal services — and returns extracted title/content to the anonymous caller. GENERAL_URL_REGEX (lib/url-detector.ts:38) happily matches internal hosts.
findings.fix: Validate the GET param with FetchUrlSchema's HttpUrl; in the fetcher, resolve the host and reject private/loopback/link-local ranges (and re-check after redirects) before fetching.

findings.title: Budget bypass: unauthenticated Anthropic call, session fetched but never enforced
findings.impact: route.ts:9-10 reads the session then ignores it — `user` is unused and there is no 401. Anyone can POST {prompt} and burn haiku tokens (direct api.anthropic.com fetch, no checkBudget). Per-call cost is capped (haiku, 500 tokens) but per-user spend is unmetered and loopable. Prompt is also interpolated raw into the LLM message at :33 with no length cap.
findings.fix: Add the same 401 guard as sibling idea-factory/agent/route.ts:15-17, add a zod schema (prompt: z.string().min(1).max(N)), and gate through checkBudget before the fetch.

findings.title: Budget bypass: authed route fans out up to 20 Anthropic calls per POST with no budget gate
findings.impact: Each POST loops the user's 20 most recent completed queries and calls extractTopics for each — one haiku call apiece via lib/side-canal.ts callLLM (side-canal.ts:26). No checkBudget/tier gating anywhere on the path, so any logged-in user can loop the endpoint for 20x-amplified spend per request. Mitigations: auth required, haiku model, 500 max_tokens per call, OpenRouter free fallback — hence HIGH not BLOCKER.
findings.fix: Run checkBudget (as simple-query does at ~:270) before the extraction loop, and/or debounce per-user (e.g. one re-extract per N minutes recorded in DB).

findings.title: Unauthenticated, un-budget-gated Anthropic call (budget bypass)
findings.impact: Anyone without a session can invoke callProvider('anthropic') (Haiku, 500 tokens/call) in a loop. No checkBudget (only simple-query has it, at app/api/simple-query/route.ts:277), no rate limit, no auth rejection — direct anonymous cost-burn and quota-drain vector against the production Anthropic key.
findings.fix: Gate the paid call behind checkBudget(user) from lib/token-budget.ts (reject or downgrade anonymous users to the free OpenRouter path), and add per-IP rate limiting reusing the app/api/query/rate-limit.ts helper already used by simple-query.

findings.title: Anonymous POST triggers up to two ungated Anthropic calls
findings.impact: extractTopics (:41) and fire-and-forget generateSynopsis (:55) both reach fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages') via lib/side-canal.ts callLLM (:26) with no budget check and no auth rejection — 2x paid calls per anonymous request, amplifying the cost-burn surface beyond quick-query.
findings.fix: Require a session (or at minimum run checkBudget) before calling extractTopics/generateSynopsis; make side-canal spend count against the user's token budget.

findings.title: Duplicate extract handler doubles the ungated paid-LLM surface
findings.impact: POST /api/side-canal is a near-verbatim copy of /api/side-canal/extract with the same ungated Anthropic calls (extractTopics :72, generateSynopsis :86) — two anonymous endpoints for the same paid function, and a drift hazard (extract gained legendMode, this one did not).
findings.fix: Delete the POST handler here (or make it delegate to the extract route's logic) and apply the same auth/budget gate as the extract fix.

findings.title: Anonymous synopsis generation reaches paid Anthropic call
findings.impact: generateSynopsis -> callLLM -> api.anthropic.com (lib/side-canal.ts:260 -> 26) fires with no auth rejection and no checkBudget; anonymous callers can burn credits whenever legacy user_id-NULL query_topics rows exist for a topicId, and each request inserts a synopses row with user_id NULL.
findings.fix: Require a valid session and run checkBudget before generateSynopsis; scope the topic lookup to the authenticated user.

findings.title: Settings route has no auth: leaks masked API key material and accepts anonymous config writes
findings.impact: Unauthenticated GET returns first-4 + last-4 real characters of the server's ANTHROPIC/DEEPSEEK/XAI/OPENROUTER keys (maskApiKey :103-106) — 4 real trailing chars per key to anyone. Unauthenticated POST (:50-101) writes attacker-supplied plaintext keys/model config into the shared module-global settingsStore, poisoning what every user sees on GET. Store is not exported so runtime blast radius is currently contained, but this is a settings surface with zero auth at launch.
findings.fix: Add the same getUserFromSession 401 gate used by profile routes to both GET and POST (admin-only ideally); stop echoing any key material — return boolean 'configured' flags instead of masked keys.

findings.title: Unauthenticated public endpoint triggers paid Anthropic synthesis — budget/rate-limit/auth bypass
findings.impact: Any anonymous client can POST /api/tot-consensus directly and burn a MODELS.premium Anthropic call (max_tokens 4096, streamSynthesis at line 378 or direct fetch at line 385) per request, plus 3-6 OpenRouter free-tier advisor calls. No getUserFromSession, no checkRateLimit, no checkBudget anywhere in the file — the daily-token-budget system in simple-query (route.ts:277) is trivially bypassed by skipping the front door. Scriptable cost-drain against the app's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
findings.fix: This route exists as an internal contract from simple-query (route.ts:312). Either (a) require a valid session + run checkRateLimit/checkBudget at the top like simple-query does, or (b) make it internal-only: require a shared-secret header set by the simple-query proxy and 404 external calls. Option (b) matches the 'INTERNAL contract' comment at line 25.

findings.title: Auth check reads wrong cookie name ('session' vs 'session_token') — owners of their own queries always get 401
findings.impact: Sessions are set as 'session_token' (app/api/auth/email/route.ts:152, dev-login:48) but this route reads cookies.get('session'). Browser EventSource (compare/[id]/page.tsx:57, useVerificationStream.ts:89) cannot send Authorization headers, so for any user-owned query the token is always undefined → 401 'Authentication required'. The ownership gate has never passed in production via a browser; the SSE feature is dead for signed-in users, and the fact nobody noticed means these flows only ever exercised anonymous queries.
findings.fix: Change line 14 to request.cookies.get('session_token')?.value (keep the Bearer fallback). Add one integration test that streams a user-owned query with the real cookie name.

findings.title: web-browse: unauthenticated paid-LLM driver, no budget gate
findings.impact: POST /api/web-browse has NO auth and NO checkBudget. Any anonymous caller submits a URL and the handler fetches it and pipes up to 15k chars into callProvider('anthropic', MODELS.budget=Haiku, maxTokens 2000) at lines 173/222/302. Unlimited unmetered Anthropic spend by anonymous users; also an SSRF surface (schema enforces http(s) only but there is no localhost/private-IP block on the server-side fetch).
findings.fix: Require a session (reject if no userId) and run checkBudget(user) before any callProvider call, mirroring simple-query/route.ts:277. Add a private-IP/localhost denylist to the fetch to close SSRF.

findings.title: tree-chat: unauthenticated Sonnet-5 call, no budget gate
findings.impact: POST /api/tree-chat reads the session but never rejects (userId defaults to null, line 41-43) and calls anthropic.messages.create with MODELS.mid (Sonnet-5), max_tokens 800, on arbitrary user text. No checkBudget anywhere. Anonymous callers burn Sonnet tokens with no daily cap.
findings.fix: Reject unauthenticated requests and call checkBudget(user) before anthropic.messages.create, as simple-query does.

findings.title: x-video-analysis: auth'd but bypasses daily token budget
findings.impact: Route requires login (good, lines 54-60) but never calls checkBudget before anthropic.messages.create with MODELS.mid (Sonnet-5) vision, max_tokens 2048 (line 112). A logged-in free-tier user can call Sonnet vision repeatedly with the daily token budget never enforced.
findings.fix: Call checkBudget(user) after deriving userId and return 429/daily_budget_exceeded when !allowed, before the vision call.

findings.title: BTCPay webhook signature verification fails OPEN when secret unset
findings.impact: btcPay.verifyWebhook returns true when BTCPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not configured ('Allow in development', lines 170-173). The btcpay webhook (route.ts:38) then treats any forged request as valid and calls addTokens/upsertSubscription — free tokens and subscriptions if the secret is ever missing in production. Contrast crypto webhook's verifyIPN which throws (fails closed).
findings.fix: Return false (reject) when the secret is unset, or hard-fail at boot if BTCPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is missing in production. Never grant on an unverifiable signature.

findings.title: Unauthenticated SSRF through main query flow (content-derived URL auto-fetched)
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: Missing SSRF guard at the shared webpage-fetch sink (root cause)
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: X-OAuth sessions never expire — expires_at written as ISO string into an INTEGER column
findings.impact: x/callback stores `expires_at = new Date(...).toISOString()` (a text like '2026-08-11T...') into sessions.expires_at, which is declared INTEGER NOT NULL (lib/db/schema.ts:26). SQLite keeps the non-numeric text as TEXT. validateSession compares `s.expires_at > strftime('%s','now')` (lib/db/auth.ts:183); strftime returns a numeric string ('1752...'). The comparison is then lexical: an ISO string beginning '20xx' always sorts greater than the 10-digit unix-seconds string, so the row is ALWAYS considered non-expired and cleanExpiredSessions() (lib/db/auth.ts:249) never deletes it. Net effect: a stolen/leaked X-login session cookie is valid forever — the intended 30-day cap is not enforced for this provider. (All other providers store integer seconds and expire correctly.)
findings.fix: Store unix seconds like the other routes: `Math.floor((Date.now()+30*24*60*60*1000)/1000)`. Also provide the `id` PK (x/callback and dev-login omit it; email/route.ts:144 sets it correctly).

findings.title: pageContext resends the last 5 messages that conversationHistory already carries — 100% duplicated, ~2-4K tokens/query
findings.impact: In any expanded chat (every turn after the first), getPageContext() returns the FULL text of the last 5 messages (useHomePageDerived.ts:106-112). The server then sends those same messages twice to the model: conversationHistory.slice(-6) becomes the messages array (route.ts:460-463), and pageContext is appended wholesale to dynamicContext (route.ts:536-537), which is the UNCACHED block (multi-provider-api.ts:80 — no cache_control). Typical assistant answers here run 2-6K chars (SYNTHESIS + [RELATED]/[NEXT] sections are mandated by the prompt), so 5 messages ≈ 8-15K chars ≈ 2,000-4,000 duplicated input tokens per query, at full $5/M Opus input rate = ~$0.01-0.02/query, on essentially 100% of conversation traffic. It also pollutes the cache key (query-cache.ts:100 hashes pageContext), so no two mid-conversation queries ever share a cache entry.
findings.fix: When the chat is expanded, send pageContext: undefined (history already covers it) — keep the DOM-scrape branch only for the non-chat landing view. One-line client change in useHomePageDerived.ts:106; alternatively gate server-side in route.ts step 6 when conversationHistory is non-empty.

findings.title: sideCanalContext (synopses + grimoire + refinements) injected twice per query, and the synopsis source is unbounded — 3K to 50K+ tokens/query on matched topics
findings.impact: The same sideCanalContext string is sent to the model twice: (a) route.ts:464-471 injects it as an assistant message; (b) runFusionPipeline passes it as contextInjection into fuseIntelligence (query-fusion.ts:162-163 → intelligence-fusion.ts:126), and generateEnhancedSystemPrompt appends it under 'CONTEXT FROM PREVIOUS CONVERSATIONS:' (intelligence-fusion-behaviors.ts:231-235), which applyFusionToPrompt puts into dynamicContext (route.ts:524-525). Grimoire instructions + up to 10 memories and live refinements ride the same doubled string (query-fusion.ts:109-148). Worse, the source is unbounded: getContextForQuery returns ALL synopses for up to 3 matched topics with no LIMIT (side-canal.ts:354-368), and synopses append forever — INSERT OR REPLACE with a fresh random id never replaces (side-canal.ts:264-270), one new row per query on the topic (query-response-builder.ts:89-90). Production DB (data/akhai.db) has 964 synopses; top topic = 94 synopses / 55,692 chars ≈ 14K tokens. A query matching that topic injects ~14K tokens ONCE and ~28K after doubling; matching the top 3 topics ≈ 26K → 52K tokens ≈ $0.26/query at Opus rates. Even a mid-tier 10-synopsis topic wastes ~3K tokens/query (half pure duplication).
findings.fix: Three surgical changes: (1) stop passing contextInjection into fuseIntelligence (query-fusion.ts:162 — pass null) OR drop the assistant-message injection at route.ts:464-471, keeping exactly one injection point; (2) add ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 3 (per topic, or ~5 total) to the synopses SELECT in side-canal.ts:354-362 and cap the joined string at ~2K chars; (3) make synopses actually replace: key INSERT OR REPLACE on topic_id (or delete old rows for the topic before insert) at side-canal.ts:264-270.

findings.title: Non-streamed synthesis fetch to Anthropic has NO timeout — headers and body both unbounded
findings.impact: This is the fallback whenever streamSynthesis returns null (any stream flap, 529, abort) and the default for callers without queryId. A hung socket or trickling body (fetch line 385, json() line 401) hangs the tot route indefinitely. Compounded: simple-query's internal proxy fetch to this route (app/api/simple-query/route.ts:312) also has no signal, so the whole user query hangs until platform kill; the query row is left for the stale-pending scavenger. Exact 69d6f3d class — the same file's advisor calls (line 107-134) got the fix, this fetch didn't.
findings.fix: Add signal: AbortSignal.timeout(90_000) to the synthesis fetch at line 385 (mirror synthesis-stream.ts:59-60), and a signal (~120s) on the internal tot proxy fetch at simple-query/route.ts:312.

findings.title: User-pasted URL visiting in the main query hot path has zero timeouts (10 fetches)
findings.impact: simple-query/route.ts:180 awaits visitURLs(query) → fetchMultipleURLs → fetches with no signal: url-content-fetcher.ts:92 and :114 (GitHub API) and :185 (arbitrary user URL, then unbounded res.text()); url-content-parsers.ts has SEVEN fetches and zero AbortSignal in the entire file (lines 72, 119, 143, 169, 297, 343, 384 — oembed, YouTube transcript, fxtwitter/nitter/vxtwitter). Any tarpit or slow-loris URL a user pastes stalls their entire query until the platform kills the function. Same pattern in secondary routes: app/api/x-video-analysis/route.ts:28 and lib/tools/youtube-fetcher.ts:59, lib/tools/x-thread-fetcher.ts:75.
findings.fix: Add AbortSignal.timeout(8-10s) to every fetch in url-content-fetcher.ts and url-content-parsers.ts (and the tools/ fetchers); optionally wrap fetchMultipleURLs in an overall Promise deadline so 3 URLs can't serialize 3 hangs.

findings.title: callOpenRouter hardcodes a :free slug the repo's own comments record as timing out 3/3, ignores request.model, and has no auto-router fallback
findings.impact: The anonymous lane (simple-query/route.ts:385-388 sets MODELS.free = 'meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free', models.ts:7) and the signed-in openrouter fallback both route through callOpenRouter, which silently discards request.model and pins 'nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free' (lines 620, 647) — the exact slug models.ts:18-19 notes 'timed out 3/3 runs 2026-07-10 @35s/0 tok'. Unlike openrouter.ts:61 (tot advisors) there is no free-auto-router retry, and free slugs rotate weekly (openrouter.ts:42). Worse, the resulting 180s AbortSignal timeout message matches simple-query's shouldRetry ('timeout', line 834) → 3 attempts × 180s ≈ 9 min for a signed-out user whose only fallback is openrouter itself (line 853).
findings.fix: Honor request.model in callOpenRouter (use MODELS.free), and add the callFreeChatWithFallback-style one-shot auto-router ('openrouter/free') retry on 404/429/402/400-model, matching lib/openrouter.ts:46-77.

findings.title: tot success path never emits a terminal SSE event — buffer + client EventSource leak on every consensus query
findings.impact: The tot branch emits stage 'analysis' (deliberately non-terminal, lines 339-354) then returns at line 356. The comment at 342-344 claims 'Real terminal is the route-level complete at the end' but the return exits POST before line 1021 ever runs, and tot-consensus/route.ts contains zero emitThought terminal calls. Result per successful tot query: (1) server buffer (60-420 events, ~30-150KB) orphaned forever in thought-stream.ts buffers Map — cleanup only fires on 'complete'/'error' (thought-stream.ts:237-242); (2) client EventSource never receives a terminal stage so never closes (useQueryHandlers.ts:353-377); (3) MetadataStrip never enters its summary phase (the 'complete' event 'enables MetadataStrip summary phase' per route.ts:1020).
findings.fix: After the guard check in the tot branch (route.ts:336), emit a proper stage:'complete' event (with tokens/cost from gtpResult.metrics) immediately before the return at 356 — the guard/save work is already done at that point, so the 'closes SSE mid-pipeline' concern the comment cites no longer applies to this position.

findings.title: Client never closes EventSource in catch/finally — every failed query leaks a live SSE connection
findings.impact: evtSource is closed only on receiving a terminal event (line 376) or a transport error (382-385). When fetch throws — budget 402 (route.ts:279), no-keys 500 (route.ts:410), all-providers-failed 500 (route.ts:910), network error — the catch (line 524) and finally (line 568) leave the EventSource open. The server SSE GET succeeded, so no onerror fires; the connection stays open until page unload. Over HTTP/1.1 (dev, or non-h2 proxy) the browser's ~6-connections-per-origin cap means a handful of failed queries hang all subsequent requests to the app. Server side, each leaked connection holds a registered controller and keeps the connections Map entry alive.
findings.fix: Hoist evtSource above the try (or close it in both the catch and finally blocks): add evtSource?.close() in the finally at line 568-572.

findings.title: Production env template drifted far behind runtime reality (6 vars vs ~25 actually consumed)
findings.impact: Missing: SENTRY_DSN/NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN (Sentry made load-bearing in c5e7f9a; instrumentation.ts:12), GUARD_NLI_URL/GUARD_NLI_TOKEN (grounding-client.ts:14-15; only documented in guard-service/README.md), DEEPSEEK/MISTRAL/XAI advisor keys (GTP consensus, lib/provider-selector.ts:131-141), RESEND_API_KEY/RESEND_FROM_EMAIL (email auth), all Stripe/NOWPayments/BTCPay vars, and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL (payment callbacks nowpayments.ts:145-147, X OAuth). It instead documents NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, read only by the suspended GitHub OAuth path (lib/auth.ts:32). A fresh host configured from this template ships with GTP advisors, guard, Sentry, email auth, and payments all silently off.
findings.fix: Regenerate the template from the actual process.env surface (grep in packages/web yields the list); mark required vs optional; delete NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL or migrate lib/auth.ts:32 to NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL. Also backfill packages/web/.env.example (missing MISTRAL/XAI/DEEPSEEK/GROQ keys, GUARD_NLI_*, RESEND_FROM_EMAIL, TWITTER_REDIRECT_URI, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_CREDITS_*/TEAM/INSTINCT price IDs, NOWPAYMENTS_SANDBOX, PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS, REACT_AGENT_LIVE/F2_LIVE/SC_MULTIPATH flags).

findings.title: NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars are baked into the prod client bundle from the dev Mac's .env.local — undocumented build-time/runtime env split
findings.impact: The standalone flow builds locally, so NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN (instrumentation-client.ts:11), PostHog key, Stripe publishable key + price IDs, and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL are inlined at build time from whatever the developer's local .env.local holds; the VPS .env.local copied at line 25 only affects server runtime. Prod can silently ship with dev/absent analytics DSNs or wrong Stripe price IDs and nothing fails loudly.
findings.fix: Build against a committed-name env file (e.g. .env.production, gitignored, values mirrored from the VPS) and have the deploy script assert the critical NEXT_PUBLIC_* values are present before `next build`; document the split in the template header.

findings.title: Hardcoded FlokiNET IP 82.221.101.3 across deploy tooling — every reference breaks on the Aug-8 host move
findings.impact: quick-deploy.sh:9, quick-deploy-standalone.sh:5, and the printed CUTOVER/ROLLBACK commands (quick-deploy-standalone.sh:40-41) all pin the dead FlokiNET box; guard-service/README.md keys its ufw allowlist to the FlokiNET VPS IP. Migration requires hand-editing four places plus re-doing the guard box firewall, with no single source of truth.
findings.fix: Read the host from one place (VPS_HOST env var or deploy/config file) in all scripts and docs; add the guard-box ufw update to the migration checklist.

findings.title: Instinct ($200/mo) and Team ($40/user/mo) sell features that do not exist
findings.impact: Instinct promises 'Extended context (200K)', 'Custom methodologies', 'Research artifacts', 'API access' (lines 51-56); Team promises 'Team workspace', 'Shared mind maps', 'Collaboration tools', 'Admin dashboard' (lines 67-72). Grep across app/, components/, lib/ finds these strings only in pricing/stripe copy — no team workspace, no sharing, no API-key management UI, no context-size gating by tier exists. Users can pay today (Stripe + crypto buttons are live on /pricing) for capabilities that are pure vaporware.
findings.fix: Remove or clearly mark unbuilt features ('coming soon'), or hide the Instinct/Team cards until the features ship. For a freeze gate, disable purchase on plans whose feature list is not implemented.

findings.title: "We do not use tracking cookies" contradicted by PostHog cookie persistence
findings.impact: Privacy policy states 'We do not use tracking cookies.' posthog.ts:33 initializes with `persistence: 'localStorage+cookie'`, which sets a ph_* identifier cookie in the browser — the config comment even claims 'Cookieless tracking (privacy-friendly)' while configuring the opposite. Session recording options are also configured (posthog.ts:39-43) and never disabled, so if replay is enabled in the PostHog project, users are recorded while the policy promises only 'basic usage analytics'.
findings.fix: Set `persistence: 'localStorage'` (or 'memory') and `disable_session_recording: true`, or amend the privacy policy to disclose the cookie and recording behavior.

findings.title: "You can delete your data at any time from your profile settings" — no deletion exists
findings.impact: The only DELETE API handlers in the app are auth/social/disconnect, tree-config/presets, and debug — there is no endpoint to delete queries, conversations, or the account, and the history API (app/api/history) has no DELETE. The settings page's 'clear all data' button (settings/page.tsx:321-329) calls clearAllData which only runs `localStorage.clear()` (lib/stores/settings-store.ts:276-281); every server-side query row survives. The policy's core data-rights promise is unbacked.
findings.fix: Implement server-side deletion (per-conversation and full account) wired to the profile settings UI, or change the policy to say deletion is by email request only (the 'contact us' sentence already exists at line 31).

findings.title: Privacy toggles are theater: 'history save/off' and 'analytics on/off' control nothing
findings.impact: The settings UI offers privacy.saveHistory (line 295) and privacy.analytics (line 309) toggles. Grep shows settings.privacy.saveHistory is consumed nowhere in the query/persistence path (simple-query always writes to the queries table), and no PostHog code (posthog.ts, analytics.ts, providers.tsx) reads the analytics flag — capture continues after the user opts out. For a sovereignty-branded product, non-functional privacy controls are worse than absent ones.
findings.fix: Honor saveHistory in the query persistence path and call posthog.opt_out_capturing() when analytics is off — or remove the toggles until they work.

findings.title: "Claude Opus 4.5" claimed on every plan/credit card; actual models are Opus 4.8/Sonnet 5/Haiku 4.5/free Llama
findings.impact: 'Claude Opus 4.5' appears on the pricing header (pricing/page.tsx:82), the Free and Pro plan cards (pricing-config.ts:19,35), and all four credit cards (pricing/page.tsx:228,262,297,331). Actual routing (lib/models.ts:3-6) is claude-opus-4-8 (premium), claude-sonnet-5 (mid), claude-haiku-4-5 (budget) selected per query by provider-selector — and signed-out users are routed exclusively to the free OpenRouter Llama model (simple-query/route.ts:383-387). The claim is wrong on version and wrong that every query runs on Opus at all.
findings.fix: Replace the model name with honest copy ('Claude Opus-class routing' or the real tier table), or single-source the display name from lib/models.ts so it can never drift again.

findings.title: GTP consensus card names advisors that are not used: "DeepSeek, Mistral, Grok"
findings.impact: The methodology explorer on the landing page (LogoSection renders METHODOLOGY_DETAILS) tells users 'Round 1: DeepSeek, Mistral, Grok analyze independently'. The actual advisors are three free OpenRouter models: openai/gpt-oss-120b, cohere/north-mini-code, meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b (lib/models.ts:16-22; tot-consensus/route.ts:48-64, 'free-only by decision'). The privacy page (line 21) discloses the real advisors correctly — the marketing card contradicts the privacy page inside the same app. The tot card also mislabels itself 'Tree of Thoughts (Yao et al., NeurIPS 2023)' while describing a multi-model consensus, which is a different technique.
findings.fix: Update methodology-data.ts line 294 to name the real advisor labs (or 'three independent frontier-lab models'), and align the card description with what the tot-consensus route actually does.

findings.title: cod system prompt contradicts itself: 'SHORT, 2-4 paragraphs, no meta-commentary, no summarizing' then demands 3 drafts + reflection + a 5-line summary
findings.impact: writingStyle (line 286) orders 'SHORT. Only necessary words... Prefer 2-4 paragraphs over 10... No meta-commentary about your own response... No "To summarize"'; the cod block (line 308) then requires two full drafts plus a [REFLECTION] (meta-commentary by definition), and synthesisSection (line 293) requires a trailing 5-sentence summary. Plus two competing structure schemas in one prompt: UNIVERSAL_STRUCTURE_INSTRUCTION '# Title/## Subheading' (prompts/structure-instruction.ts:1-14) vs bracket-tag [DRAFT 1] format. The model resolves the conflict arbitrarily — output format and length are unstable across runs.
findings.fix: For the cod case, drop or override the brevity/no-summary clauses and pick ONE structural schema (bracket tags or markdown headings, not both).

findings.title: 2000-token ceiling truncates cod's most valuable content first (FINAL ANSWER is last) with no truncation detection
findings.impact: The cod format puts [FINAL ANSWER], ## SYNTHESIS and [RELATED]/[NEXT] at the end; hitting max_tokens cuts exactly those, leaving the user drafts+reflection without the polished answer. multi-provider-api.ts handles only stop_reason 'refusal' (lines 252-253, 353) — max_tokens/finish_reason is never checked anywhere, so truncation is silent: no retry, no warning, answer saved as status 'complete'. Budget comment 'chain-of-draft, concise' is stale — it describes paper-CoD, not the shipped triple-draft prompt.
findings.fix: Detect stop_reason 'max_tokens' in callProvider and either continue/retry with a higher cap or flag truncation in the response; raise the cod budget if the triple-draft format is kept.

findings.title: Fallback provider path accepts empty completions — saved as status 'complete' with empty answer
findings.impact: Primary path re-rolls on empty content ('PROVIDER_NO_CONTENT 529', route.ts:816); the fallback loop (route.ts:864-877) has no such check, so a fallback provider returning ''/whitespace sets fallbackSucceeded=true, content='' (line 935), and the query is persisted complete (lines 1006-1016) with an empty finalAnswer. Affects all methodologies including cod whenever the primary provider fails first.
findings.fix: Apply the same empty-content check inside the fallback try (throw to continue to the next provider in the chain).

findings.title: executeProgramOfThought is 542 LOC of dead code with zero tests
findings.impact: Only references are the export (core/src/index.ts:129) and registry.ts:107; METHODOLOGY_REGISTRY's execute functions are never invoked anywhere in the repo, and the web route never imports core methodologies. No test file covers pas.ts (core/tests has cod/gtp/integration only; the sole 'pas' grep hit in gtp-validation.ts:43 is the word 'passed'). methodology-runner.ts (web) with its pas timeout entry (line 23) also has no consumers. Registry stats report it 'implemented' (registry.ts:118), inflating the 7-methodology claim.
findings.fix: Either delete pas.ts + registry entry (and the runner) or add tests and an actual execution path. For the freeze audit, mark it 'planned/not wired' in registry.ts so status reporting is honest.

findings.title: METHOD_PLAIN keys 'pot' but the methodology id is 'pas' — plain-language label never shows, 'pot' key is dead
findings.impact: METHOD_PLAIN has pot: 'Calculation' (thought-stream.ts:164) but every id source emits 'pas' (schema.ts:10 zod enum, provider-selector.ts:10 type, query-pipeline.ts:206 selector, intelligence-fusion-scoring.ts:193). query-fusion.ts:217 and :250-251 do METHOD_PLAIN[selectedMethod.id] ?? selectedMethod.id → users see 'Thinking method: pas' in the live panel for every math query, defeating the simple-terms design; the 'pot' entry is unreachable.
findings.fix: Rename the key: pas: 'Calculation' (one-line edit in thought-stream.ts:164).

findings.title: Core PoT loop wastes ~2x tokens: isFinal regex tests for phrases the prompt never asks for; prose final answers are discarded
findings.impact: Termination requires the reasoning to match /final answer|result is|answer:|solution:/ (pas.ts:452), but the mandated format (pas.ts:413-418) only specifies 'Reasoning:/Code:' — so iteration 1 rarely finalizes and line 295 forces a second full round-trip. Each iteration re-sends the ~450-token few-shot system prompt plus the monotonically growing context (pas.ts:186-193, 221, 255, 274), uncached. If the model answers the follow-up in prose without a code block ('Yes, the answer is 42'), lines 224-229 discard it and demand code again — finalAnswer can only ever be raw stdout (pas.ts:278), so the returned answer is a bare number with no synthesis. Off-by-one at pas.ts:277 (iteration >= maxIterations-1) force-finalizes at iteration 4 of 5.
findings.fix: If keeping the impl: instruct the model to emit an explicit FINAL marker, accept a prose final answer as terminal, add an answer-synthesis step, and fix the -1 off-by-one. Moot if pas.ts is deleted.

findings.title: Python config branch is a guaranteed-failure token trap
findings.impact: PoTConfig.language accepts 'python' (pas.ts:32) and buildSystemPrompt ships full Python few-shot examples (pas.ts:384-404), but executeCode throws unconditionally for python (pas.ts:469-471). Configured with python, every one of up to 5 iterations pays for an LLM call, then hits 'Execution Error: Python execution not yet implemented... Please fix the code' (pas.ts:313) — the model can never fix it — ending in 'Unable to determine answer' (pas.ts:340). ~20 lines of unreachable prompt examples.
findings.fix: Remove 'python' from the type union and delete the python example branch until an executor exists (fail at config time, not after 5 paid calls).

findings.title: React output-token budget is never applied on the live agent path
findings.impact: generateText has no maxOutputTokens (only stopWhen: stepCountIs(5) and an 85s abort at react-agent.ts:66). OUTPUT_BUDGETS.react = 2500 (packages/web/lib/output-budgets.ts:7) only governs callProvider branches, so the production react path — the one running Opus premium — has no output ceiling at all; worst-case cost protection the budgets file promises ('These are CEILINGS... worst-case cost protection') doesn't exist here.
findings.fix: Pass maxOutputTokens: maxTokensFor('react') (or a per-step cap) into the generateText call in runReactAgent.

findings.title: Agent failure degrades silently and burns unrecorded Opus tokens
findings.impact: When runReactAgent throws (85s timeout, REACT_AGENT_NO_CONTENT at route.ts:655, Anthropic outage), the catch at 845 seeds triedProviders with 'anthropic' (854) and falls back to a non-Anthropic provider on the knowledge-recall prompt. The user already saw 'Searching the web...' (route.ts:625) and per-step search events, then receives an answer with zero sources and no downgrade notice. Tokens the partially-run agent consumed are never recorded — recordCall at route.ts:679 sits after the throw point — contradicting the E5 comment's 'full cost recording (COGS)' claim for the failure half of the path.
findings.fix: Emit an explicit 'live search failed — answering from model knowledge' event before falling back; wrap runReactAgent so partial usage (when available from the SDK error/steps) is recorded with outcome:'error'.

findings.title: Auto web search runs and is discarded on the react-agent path; hardcoded localhost fetch with no timeout
findings.impact: Lines 490-507 run a Brave/DDG search for EVERY query and inject results into dynamicContext — but the react-agent branch (618-699) never reads dynamicContext. Each react query burns 1 wasted Brave call (quota is 2000/mo free, per the E5 comment at route.ts:615) plus serial latency (DDG throttle is 3s, fetch has no timeout) before the agent then does up to 5 more searches. The URL is hardcoded 'http://localhost:3000/api/web-search' — breaks under any other port/host and is an HTTP round-trip when webSearchCore() is directly importable (it exists precisely to avoid this, web-search-core.ts:4-6).
findings.fix: Skip the auto-search when selectedMethod.id === 'react' && REACT_AGENT_LIVE path will run; call webSearchCore() directly instead of fetching localhost for the remaining paths.

findings.title: Core executeReAct is dead code with placeholder tools, still exported as public API
findings.impact: No runtime consumer exists: web uses lib/react-agent.ts, mcp-server has zero react references, the registry entry (packages/core/src/methodologies/registry.ts:91) is imported only by core/src/index.ts. Yet executeReAct is publicly exported (packages/core/src/index.ts:109) with searchTool returning hardcoded placeholder text ('[Note: This is a placeholder...]', tools/search.ts:~40-65) and lookupTool a 4-term hardcoded dictionary (tools/lookup.ts:~44-55). Any external consumer of @akhai/core gets a 'ReAct' whose observations are junk. Zero tests cover it. Its header claim 'Enables dynamic information seeking' (react.ts:10) is false as shipped.
findings.fix: For the freeze gate: delete core react.ts + tools/ subtree and the index/registry exports, or mark the export @deprecated with a pointer to the web agent. Do not leave placeholder tools in a public package API.

findings.title: Entire core sc.ts (611 LOC executeBufferOfThoughts) is dead code at runtime; registry metadata describes an implementation nobody runs
findings.impact: Only callers are core's own registry.ts:9,75 and index.ts:100-102 — no web/api/mcp-server import (web implements sc independently in query-pipeline.ts:311 + sc-multipath.ts; commit c497ce6 confirms core has no built runtime surface for web). Registry advertises avgLatencyMs 18000 / costPer1K 0.006 / 'thought buffering and distillation' (registry.ts:80-87) for a code path that never executes, misleading anyone reading METHODOLOGY_REGISTRY as system truth.
findings.fix: Either delete sc.ts + registry entry execute pointer (mark status accordingly), or wire the registry to the real web implementation. At minimum annotate the registry entry as non-runtime.

findings.title: Served sc prompt self-contradicts and mandates redundant sections — output-token waste on every sc answer
findings.impact: writingStyle (query-pipeline.ts:286) commands 'SHORT... Prefer 2-4 paragraphs... No summary of what you just said... No To summarize', while synthesisSection (query-pipeline.ts:292) mandates ending EVERY response with '## SYNTHESIS' + exactly 5 summary sentences, plus enhancementSection (:289) adds [RELATED]/[NEXT]. Combined with the sc format, every answer must emit 7 mandatory sections ([BUFFER][REASONING][VALIDATION][ANSWER] + SYNTHESIS + RELATED + NEXT); VALIDATION→ANSWER→SYNTHESIS restates the same content three times. Output tokens are ~4-5x input price (output-budgets.ts:1), so the model resolves a direct instruction conflict unpredictably AND burns the expensive token class on ceremony. Affects all methodologies sharing these blocks.
findings.fix: Resolve the contradiction explicitly (e.g., 'SYNTHESIS is the one permitted summary') and collapse VALIDATION/ANSWER duplication in the sc block; consider making RELATED/NEXT optional for short answers.

findings.title: Round-2 skip threshold (consensus ≥ 0.85) is mathematically unreachable — round 2 always fires on full round 1
findings.impact: calculateConsensus = 0.5 + overlap*0.5 where overlap requires verbatim lowercase-identical key-point strings across DIFFERENT models (consensus-scoring.ts:48-57); hitting 0.85 needs 70% verbatim duplication, which effectively never happens. So the 'if needed' cross-pollination is unconditional whenever all 3 advisors succeed: +3 calls (doubling load on the shared ~20 req/min free tier, route.ts:42-43), ~1.1K redundant prompt tokens (3 × ~1500-char round-1 context, line 299), +35s latency. The finalConsensus surfaced to clients (line 472) is likewise decorative — it hovers near 0.5 regardless of actual agreement, as is calculateConfidence's keyword count.
findings.fix: Either drop the pretense (always run round 2 and delete the dead gate + decorative metric) or implement a real agreement measure (embedding/judge-based) before displaying 'consensus %' to users.

findings.title: Synthesis cost computed at $3/$15 per M but MODELS.premium is claude-opus-4-8 ($5/$25) — 40% COGS understatement, outside the SHIELD drift guard
findings.impact: The route hardcodes anthropic {input:0.003, output:0.015}/1K (line 437) while synthesis runs MODELS.premium='claude-opus-4-8' (lib/models.ts:3), which the project's own MODEL_RATES prices at 0.005/0.025 (provider-selector.ts:255, comment: 'the flat anthropic rate mispriced every model'). check-rate-drift.mjs compares only core pricing.ts vs provider-selector.ts — this third hardcoded table drifts silently. Every tot query's stored cost (simple-query updateQuery cost, line 331) is ~40% low.
findings.fix: Replace the hardcoded costs map with getProviderPricing('anthropic', MODELS.premium) from provider-selector.ts, or extend check-rate-drift.mjs to cover this file.

findings.title: tot success path never emits a terminal SSE stage — comment is wrong, thought-stream buffers leak
findings.impact: The tot branch emits stage:'analysis' (lines 339-354, whose comment claims 'Real terminal is the route-level complete at the end') then returns at 356 — the route-level 'complete' at line 1021 is unreachable for tot. Consequences: (a) thought-stream cleanup only fires on 'complete'/'error' (thought-stream.ts:237-241) and the GET cancel deletes connections but not buffers (app/api/thought-stream/route.ts:57), so every successful tot query permanently leaks its event backlog (up to 600 events incl. advisor excerpts + synthesis chunks) in module memory; (b) the live panel never receives the Done/metadata summary event the standard path sends.
findings.fix: Emit a stage:'complete' event (with tot metrics) in the tot branch before returning, matching the standard path's line 1021 emit.

findings.title: Non-streamed synthesis fallback fetch has no timeout; neither route sets maxDuration
findings.impact: streamSynthesis carefully bounds its whole body read with a 90s AbortController (synthesis-stream.ts:60-62, citing 'the 69d6f3d lesson'), but when it returns null the fallback fetch to api.anthropic.com (route.ts:385-398) has no AbortController at all — a stalled response hangs the route indefinitely. Worst-case tot chain is already 35s (round 1) + 35s (round 2) + 90s (stream) + unbounded fallback; neither tot-consensus nor simple-query exports maxDuration, and simple-query's internal fetch to tot-consensus (line 312) is also unbounded.
findings.fix: Wrap the fallback fetch in the same 90s AbortController pattern; export appropriate maxDuration from both routes and consider an abort on the simple-query→tot-consensus proxy fetch.

findings.title: tot's only PAID call (Anthropic synthesis) is never recorded to the COGS scorecard; trackUsage skipped for tot
findings.impact: recordCall rows exist for every $0 advisor call (openrouter.ts:81-102) and for the standard path (multi-provider-api.ts:137/152/359), but neither streamSynthesis nor the non-streamed synthesis fetch (tot-consensus/route.ts:385-413) records a COGS row, and the simple-query tot branch skips trackUsage (only the standard path calls it, simple-query/route.ts:1017). The reconciliation ledger shows $0 Anthropic spend for the most expensive methodology — the observability lane's 'reconciling COGS row' discipline (route.ts:156 comment) is broken exactly where money is spent.
findings.fix: Add a recordCall (purpose 'tot-synthesis', real usage + cost) after synthesis in tot-consensus/route.ts, and call trackUsage in the simple-query tot branch.

findings.title: Entire core GTP package (2,078 LOC) is product-dead; registry stores an uncallable class; advertised 'quorum early-exit' never exits early
findings.impact: packages/core/src/methodologies/tot/ (index.ts 406, FlashBroadcaster 348, FlashContextBuilder 453, LivingDatabase 566, QuorumManager 305 LOC) has zero product callers — web reimplements tot in app/api/tot-consensus/route.ts and imports @akhai/core for types only; the only consumers are core tests (tests/gtp-integration.test.ts, tests/gtp-validation.ts). registry.ts:123 stores the GTPExecutor CLASS in an `execute: Function` slot with the comment 'Wrapped in class, need to adapt' — invoking it as a function would throw. Registry.ts:128/135 advertises 'quorum early-exit (TUMIX-inspired)', but GTPExecutor awaits Promise.allSettled of ALL advisors (FlashBroadcaster.ts:82, index.ts:169) before checking quorum — the onQuorumReached callback (index.ts:161-164) alters no control flow, so early exit does not exist even in the dead implementation. Two divergent implementations of the flagship methodology is a maintenance trap at freeze.
findings.fix: Delete packages/core/src/methodologies/tot/ and the registry tot execute wiring (keep metadata if the registry is displayed), or explicitly quarantine it as reference-only; correct the 'early exit' claim.

findings.title: Live ReAct agent path ignores layer weights and fusion prompt
findings.impact: runReactAgent(query, undefined, cb) — signature is (query, model, onStep) (lib/react-agent.ts:48). No systemPrompt/dynamicContext parameter exists, so the fusion enhancement built at line 525 never reaches the agent. This is the DEFAULT for react + signed-in users (REACT_AGENT_LIVE !== '0' gate, line 618). All 11 layer weights change nothing in the agent's provider calls.
findings.fix: Thread the fusion enhancement (or at least the dominant-layer behavioral lines) into runReactAgent's system prompt, or document/indicate that react bypasses layer calibration.

findings.title: 0.3 weight floor makes 0–30% weights indistinguishable and produces contradictory prompts
findings.impact: const weight = Math.max(0.3, rawWeight) — for dominance, PRIMARY FOCUS, methodology scoring, path activations and thinking budget, weights 0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3 are identical. Worse: the behavioral text uses the RAW weight while PRIMARY FOCUS uses the floored effectiveWeight, so a layer the user set to 0% with 2+ keyword matches (e.g. Discriminator on 'critique the risks and limitations of X': activation 1.0 × 0.3 = 0.3 > 0.15) yields a prompt containing BOTH '• DISCRIMINATOR — 0% [SUPPRESS] / No critical analysis. Accept all premises.' AND 'PRIMARY FOCUS: Discriminator (30%)' (intelligence-fusion-behaviors.ts:196 vs :210). The model receives direct self-contradiction; user suppression intent is silently half-ignored.
findings.fix: Respect w=0 as a true opt-out (skip the floor when rawWeight === 0, exclude from dominantLayers), or compute PRIMARY FOCUS from raw weights so the two prompt sections can't contradict.

findings.title: Layer-derived extended thinking budget is phantom — provider hardcodes budget_tokens: 10000
findings.impact: calculateThinkingBudget (intelligence-fusion-scoring.ts:291-315) scales 3000→12000 with Meta-Core/Reasoning/Encoder effective weights, but its output (fusionResult.extendedThinkingBudget) is consumed ONLY by UI display (IntelligenceBadge.tsx:127,276 shows it as '12K tokens') and response metadata. The actual Anthropic call uses body.thinking = { type: 'enabled', budget_tokens: 10000 } unconditionally. Layer weights provably change nothing about real thinking compute; the badge advertises a budget that was never applied.
findings.fix: Pass fusionResult.extendedThinkingBudget through callProvider into body.thinking.budget_tokens (clamped < max_tokens), or stop displaying it as if it were the applied budget.

findings.title: Tree-of-Life configuration panel weights never reach the main query backend (parallel dead weight system)
findings.impact: TreeConfigurationPanel sliders (layer_weights, antipattern_suppression) save/activate only to /api/tree-config (DB). The activate callback is onConfigChange={(config) => { console.log(...); // TODO: Trigger re-analysis }} — it never writes useLayerStore, and /api/simple-query reads only useLayerStore.getState().weights (useQueryHandlers.ts:412). The active tree-config is consumed solely by /api/tree-chat (tree-chat/route.ts:46,99,104). A user tuning weights on /tree-of-life and then chatting sees zero effect — two disconnected weight stores presenting as one feature.
findings.fix: On activate, sync the config into useLayerStore (applyPreset), or clearly scope the panel to Tree Chat only in the UI.

findings.title: Consensus % measures exact-string bullet duplication, not agreement — pinned at 50%
findings.impact: Formula (lines 52-56): overlap = 1 - unique/total over lowercased key-point strings, consensus = clamp(0.5 + overlap*0.5, 0.3, 0.95). '50%' literally means 'zero extracted bullets were byte-identical across advisors' — the near-certain outcome for three different-lab LLMs paraphrasing freely. Reachable range is [0.5, 0.833] when >=2 advisors succeed (the 0.3 floor and 0.95 cap are both dead), else exactly 0. Worse, the only realistic source of nonzero overlap is shared boilerplate: extractKeyPoints also harvests bold text (line 20), and since the round-1 prompt orders every advisor to end with a confidence level (route.ts:229), identical bolded headers like '**Confidence Level: High**' (>10 chars, passes the line-26 filter) inflate 'consensus'. The number is reported as totData.finalConsensus (route.ts:472) and logged as 'Consensus: N%' (route.ts:263, 348).
findings.fix: Post-launch: replace exact-string matching with token-level Jaccard/embedding similarity between advisor answers, or drop the percentage entirely and report the honest signal that exists (n/N advisors answered). At minimum rename/relabel so 50% is not presented as 'half agreement'.

findings.title: Advisor 'confidence' is a lexical hedge-word heuristic, and it silently picks the final answer in fallback mode
findings.impact: calculateConfidence: base 0.7, +0.05 per certainty word present, -0.05 per hedge word present (binary substring includes, lines 37-42), clamped [0.3, 0.95]. 'may' substring-matches 'maybe', 'mayor', 'May 2026', and the modal 'may' is near-universal in analytic prose — a systematic -0.05. The prompt explicitly asks each model to self-report high/medium/low confidence (route.ts:229) but that self-report is ignored in favor of this scan. The value is uncalibrated decoration everywhere except route.ts:411, where with no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY the highest-'confidence' advisor's raw text becomes the entire final response — i.e., which answer the user gets is decided by who used fewer words containing 'may'.
findings.fix: For the fallback at route.ts:411, rank by something real (longest complete answer, or keyPoints count) or parse the self-reported high/medium/low the prompt already demands. Stop rendering the heuristic as a percentage bar (GTPConsensusView.tsx:146-152) if that component is ever revived.

findings.title: Zero multi-turn coverage — provider hardcodes conversationHistory: []
findings.impact: The engine's history path (simple-query route.ts:85,130,460 slices last 6 messages into the prompt) is completely uncovered. Context-recall or turn-2 contradiction regressions ship invisibly; no golden can exercise multi-turn until the provider reads a history var.
