findings.fix: Extend callApi to accept vars.history and add 2-3 multi-turn goldens (anaphora recall, cross-turn consistency).

findings.title: Guard-adversarial coverage is 2 tests hitting 2 of 4 detectors; Echo and Drift untested
findings.impact: core/src/grounding/detectors has Hype, Echo, Drift, Factuality. Floor tests cover hype ceiling (line 39) and fabricated attribution (line 47) only. Sycophantic premise-echo, topic drift, and false-premise agreement — the failure modes the '<1% hallucination' claim rests on — have zero eval pressure. Also the hype floor allows hits<=2 while the prompt itself contains 2 hype words, so echoing the user's own hype back passes (lines 44-46).
findings.fix: Add floor goldens for Echo (wrong-fact agreement bait), Drift, false-premise correction, and prompt-injection; tighten hype threshold to hits<=1 or exclude prompt-echoed words.

findings.title: Gate policy contradiction: README mandates 15/15, eval-bar passes at 85% quality (12/13)
findings.impact: README.md:9: 'all 15 must pass before router flip and before launch.' eval-bar.mjs verdict = floor AND qScore>=85, i.e. one quality golden may fail and the bar still prints PASS. Whichever is the real freeze-gate policy, one document is lying — an auditor or future dev will trust the wrong one.
findings.fix: Pick one policy; either set THRESHOLD=100 or update README to '2 floor hard-pass + >=85% quality'.

findings.title: Unauthenticated admin mutation endpoint (cross-user query reset)
findings.impact: POST /api/admin/reset-stuck has no session fetch, no auth check, and is not gated by middleware.ts (which only sets response headers). Any unauthenticated caller can flip every row with status='processing' older than 5min to status='error' across all users (marking in-flight work as failed) — a cross-user DoS. GET leaks the stuck-query count.
findings.fix: Require an authenticated admin session (validateSession + admin/role check) at the top of both handlers, or restrict the path via middleware. Return 401/403 when absent.

findings.title: GitHub OAuth callback does not validate the state parameter (login CSRF / account fixation)
findings.impact: getGitHubAuthUrl (auth.ts:44) generates a state nonce, but handleGitHubCallback(code) at callback route.ts:24 is passed only the code — state is never read back or verified against the initiating session. This defeats CSRF protection on the OAuth login and enables account-fixation attacks. The X/Twitter flow, by contrast, verifies PKCE state server-side (auth.ts:254).
findings.fix: Persist the state nonce (e.g. httpOnly cookie or server store) at initiation and verify it in the callback before exchanging the code, matching the PKCE-state pattern used by the X flow.

findings.title: Custom-credits Stripe checkout has no auth and attaches no user to metadata
findings.impact: Handler never reads session_token/getUserFromSession. It creates a Stripe session for an arbitrary $5-$10000 credit purchase with metadata { type, amount, tokens } but NO userId (route.ts:57-62). A completed payment cannot be attributed to a user by the webhook, and anyone can generate checkout sessions anonymously.
findings.fix: Read the session, require auth (or explicitly support anonymous with an email), and embed userId in the Stripe metadata so the webhook can credit the right account.

findings.title: Client-supplied body.userId lets an unauthenticated caller attribute a purchase to any user
findings.impact: userId = user?.id || body.userId || null (route.ts:37) and the same pattern in /api/checkout (route.ts:30). An unauthenticated caller can pass an arbitrary body.userId that survives to invoice/session metadata (only WARN-logged at route.ts:39). Enables identity confusion / crediting an arbitrary account on payment.
findings.fix: Do not trust body.userId; derive userId solely from the validated session for authenticated flows, and treat anonymous checkout as truly anonymous (no server-honored userId).

findings.title: unvalidated_routes ratchet has false negatives — baseline 0 is misleading
findings.impact: The check greps files containing literal 'request.json()' then passes any file matching \.parse\( — JSON.parse( satisfies it (enhanced-links/route.ts:117, depth-extract/route.ts:57), and crypto-checkout escapes scanning entirely because its param is 'req' (req.json() at route.ts:36). Three genuinely unvalidated body-reading routes in this chunk alone while the ratchet reports 0.
findings.fix: Tighten the grep to '\.json()' on any identifier and require a zod call specifically (safeParse|Schema\.parse), excluding JSON.parse; re-baseline honestly.

findings.title: Shadowed consts make recentConnections and categoryDistribution always empty
findings.impact: Inner 'const recentConnections' (line 56) and 'const categoryDistribution' (line 72) shadow the outer let declarations (lines 23-27), so the queried data is discarded and the response (lines 109-110) always returns [] for both — the dashboard preview silently shows no connections/categories despite valid data.
findings.fix: Change the inner declarations to assignments (recentConnections = db.prepare(...) / categoryDistribution = ...).

findings.title: Checkout body not zod-validated and invisible to the validation ratchet
findings.impact: Manual checks only (amount>0, truthy currency, productType enum); currency/planId/creditAmount pass unvalidated into NOWPayments params and the encoded orderId, and the file dodges the shield ratchet via the 'req' parameter name — a payment-money route is exactly where schema drift hurts.
findings.fix: Add CryptoCheckoutSchema to lib/route-schemas.ts (amount positive finite with upper bound, currency enum/regex, planId enum, creditAmount int bounds) and safeParse at line 36.

findings.title: unvalidated_routes ratchet false-negative: JSON.parse satisfies the validation regex
findings.impact: The ratchet marks a file validated if it matches `\.parse\(|\.safeParse\(|Schema|z\.object|parsed\.` anywhere. Both idea-factory/generate/route.ts (:51) and mindmap/topic-suggestions/route.ts (:121) read request.json() with zero zod yet pass because they JSON.parse the LLM's output. The unvalidated_routes=0 baseline (.shield-baselines:6) is therefore false — it masked the two worst routes in this chunk.
findings.fix: Tighten the heuristic: require the schema evidence near the request.json() call or match only `safeParse(await request.json())` / imports from lib/route-schemas; at minimum exclude bare `JSON.parse` from the match.

findings.title: No auth on implementation-tracker read/write (POST/PATCH/GET)
findings.impact: Unlike sibling dev route layer-test (prod-404 at layer-test/route.ts:38), this route has no session check and no NODE_ENV guard. Anonymous users in prod can enumerate all implementation records (GET), create records (POST :32), and validate/revert existing ones (PATCH :93-123) — data pollution and internal-progress disclosure, though payloads are zod-bounded and no paid calls occur.
findings.fix: Either require a session (401 guard) or dev-gate the whole route the way layer-test does if it is CLI-workflow-only.

findings.title: side-canal callLLM bypasses COGS instrumentation
findings.impact: Direct fetch to api.anthropic.com without recordCall (no recordCall anywhere in side-canal.ts, confirmed by grep), unlike quick-query's fallback which records. All side-canal Anthropic spend (topic extraction + synopses, potentially 2 calls per chat message) is invisible to the cogs-scorecard, undercounting real COGS.
findings.fix: Route callLLM through lib/multi-provider-api callProvider, or add recordCall with real usage/cost from the Anthropic response body.

findings.title: Unauthenticated SSE leaks live answer text and thinking deltas to anyone holding a queryId
findings.impact: No session check at all. Subscribers receive the buffered backlog plus live events, which include real generated-answer chunks (simple-query 'generating' events, route.ts:588-601) and extended-thinking deltas (560-579). queryId is client-supplied via simple-query's clientQueryId (schema.ts:29 — any string, e.g. a predictable UI-generated value), so a second user subscribing to the same id watches someone else's answer live; server ids are only 32 bits of entropy.
findings.fix: Mirror stream/[id]'s intended model: look up the query's user_id and require a matching session_token before subscribing when the query is owned; optionally enforce a minimum-entropy/format rule on clientQueryId in QuerySchema.

findings.title: Anonymous /api/stats returns GLOBAL platform metrics including total dollar spend and provider key inventory
findings.impact: getStats(null) drops the user filter (lib/db/stats.ts:30-45): any unauthenticated caller reads platform-wide query counts, total tokens, and total cost. providerStats is always global regardless of user (stats.ts:53-59), and lines 21-25 disclose exactly which provider API keys are configured. Competitive/recon data (COGS, traffic, provider mix) exposed publicly.
findings.fix: Require a session for global numbers, or return only the caller's scoped stats (pass userId and add the user filter to the usage/providerStats query); drop or auth-gate the env-key status booleans.

findings.title: Recent-queries endpoint is functionally dead — always returns an empty list
findings.impact: The route never reads the session and calls getRecentQueries(10) with no userId; the LOCK policy in lib/db/queries.ts:88-89 returns [] for no-user. RecentQueriesList.tsx:21 consumes this, so the recent-queries UI silently shows nothing for every user, signed-in or not. Fail-closed (no leak) but a shipped broken feature.
findings.fix: Read session_token → getUserFromSession like sibling routes, and pass userId: getRecentQueries(10, userId). If intentionally deprecated, delete the route and the component fetch.

findings.title: tree-config PATCH IDOR: config update/activate not scoped to owner
findings.impact: PATCH /api/tree-config validates the body but not ownership. updateTreeConfiguration (line 259) and setActiveTreeConfiguration (line 271) key on `WHERE id = ?` with no user_id constraint, so any caller (even anonymous) can rename, re-weight, or activate another user's tree configuration by guessing the numeric id.
findings.fix: Add user_id/session_id scoping to the UPDATE WHERE clauses and pass the session-derived owner from the route; reject when the config's owner doesn't match.

findings.title: upload: no auth, no rate limit, writes public files
findings.impact: POST /api/upload reads no session and enforces no rate limit; it writes caller-supplied files (10MB x 5 per request) into public/uploads (line 73) served at a public URL (line 79). Anonymous disk-fill and free file-hosting abuse.
findings.fix: Require an authenticated session, add per-user rate limiting/quota, and store outside the public web root (or behind an access check).

findings.title: presets POST unvalidated body; shield ratchet false-negative masks it
findings.impact: POST body (request.json(), line 74) is validated only by a manual `if(!name||!weights)`; `weights` is JSON.stringify'd into tree_configurations unvalidated (line 95). The shield unvalidated_routes ratchet does NOT catch this because the incidental JSON.parse(row.layer_weights) at line 51 matches its `\.parse\(` substring test, so the route is miscounted as validated and the ratchet reads 0 while a real gap exists.
findings.fix: Add a zod schema for {name, weights, description} and parse the POST body. Separately, tighten the ratchet to require a request-body validator (e.g. safeParse of the parsed json) rather than any `.parse(` substring.

findings.title: Host-rewrite SSRF via substring .replace() + attacker DNS
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: SSRF via content-derived media URL (authenticated)
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: Email verification codes logged to server console when RESEND_API_KEY unset
findings.impact: console.log(`[EMAIL AUTH] No RESEND_API_KEY — code for ${email}: ${authCode}`) runs in ANY environment where RESEND_API_KEY is missing (it is z.optional in packages/web/lib/env.ts:13, so prod boots fine without it), and the endpoint still returns success:'Verification code sent to your email'. In a misconfigured production deploy, live auth codes + emails land in server logs/PM2 output and potentially Sentry breadcrumbs — log access becomes account takeover.
findings.fix: Gate the code-printing log behind NODE_ENV !== 'production'; in production with no RESEND_API_KEY, return a 503 'email auth unavailable' instead of a fake success.

findings.title: X OAuth CSRF-state and PKCE-verifier cookies lack the secure flag
findings.impact: x_oauth_state (the CSRF token checked in x/callback:20) and x_oauth_verifier (the PKCE code_verifier — a short-lived secret) are set with only {httpOnly, maxAge:600, path} — no `secure`, no explicit `sameSite`. In production these can be transmitted over plaintext HTTP if any http entrypoint exists, exposing the PKCE verifier and the CSRF state that the callback relies on to bind the OAuth exchange. The session_token cookies elsewhere correctly set secure/sameSite; these two do not.
findings.fix: Add `secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'` (or true) and `sameSite: 'lax'` to both cookie sets in x/route.ts:41-46.

findings.title: Wallet auth message has no server-side nonce/expiry — replayable
findings.impact: generateWalletMessage embeds only a client-echoed Date.now() timestamp; wallet/verify/route.ts:22-26 extracts that timestamp FROM the client's own message and regenerates the 'expected' message from it, so the client fully controls the timestamp. There is no server-stored nonce, no freshness window check, and no single-use enforcement. Even after real signature verification is added (see BLOCKER), a captured valid signature could be replayed indefinitely to re-authenticate. This is a design gap that must be closed alongside the signature fix, not after.
findings.fix: Issue a server-generated single-use nonce (stored with a short TTL, like the PKCE verifier table), include it in the signed message, and reject reused or stale (>few-minute) nonces during verify.

findings.title: ddgCache is set-only: no eviction, no size cap, TTL checked only on read
findings.impact: Every unique DDG search string adds a permanent Map entry (results + snippets, ~1-5KB each). Enhanced-links generates ~3 AI-crafted search queries per user query, so entries accrue at ~3x query rate and expired entries are never removed (route.ts:201 TTL check on get, route.ts:252 unconditional set, zero .delete() calls in file). On a long-running server this is slow unbounded growth — tens of MB per sustained-load week, never reclaimed.
findings.fix: Cap it: reuse LRUCache from lib/intelligence-fusion-types.ts (as lib/query-cache.ts:33 does, MAX_ENTRIES + TTL), or add a periodic sweep like lib/rate-limit.ts:24.

findings.title: thought-stream buffers/connections Maps leak for queryIds that never reach a terminal stage
findings.impact: buffers and connections entries are freed ONLY 3s after a 'complete'/'error' emit (thought-stream.ts:237-242). The terminal emit lives solely in simple-query (route.ts:150/1024 complete, :1127 error). /api/tot-consensus accepts a client-supplied queryId (route.ts:188), emits reasoning/generating events under it via emitTotAdvisor/streamSynthesis, has no rate limit (only simple-query imports checkRateLimit), and never emits a terminal stage — each direct POST strands a buffer of up to 600 events (advisor excerpts, ~10s of KB) forever. Compounding: the SSE cancel handler (app/api/thought-stream/route.ts:52-60) only deletes the entry when size<=1 and never removes the departing controller from the Set, so with 2+ subscribers per queryId both cancels see size==2 and neither cleans up — dead controllers accumulate until a terminal event that may never come. Organic growth is low; abuse-driven growth is unbounded.
findings.fix: In cancel(), capture the controller in start() and delete it from the Set, then delete the entry when empty. Add a TTL sweep over buffers/connections (e.g. drop entries older than 15 min). Emit a terminal stage (or rate-limit + reject external queryIds) in /api/tot-consensus.

findings.title: settingsStore: user-saved API keys and model config silently lost on restart; per-instance divergence
findings.impact: POST /api/settings mutates a module-level object (lines 63-91) and replies "Settings saved successfully" (line 95), but nothing persists — any restart/redeploy reverts keys to process.env and consensus/model config to defaults, with no user-visible signal. In any multi-instance or serverless deploy each instance holds a different settingsStore, so reads and writes are inconsistent. Bounded in size (fixed shape) but the data-loss-on-restart is NOT acceptable for a route that claims to save; it also keeps plaintext provider keys in process memory.
findings.fix: Persist to the existing SQLite layer (lib/database.ts) or drop the POST and make settings env-only; at minimum change the response to state settings are ephemeral.

findings.title: Layer-config 'keep prompt concise' gate is dead code — all 11 layers print every query, ~300-450 uncached tokens each time
findings.impact: The condition `if (tierIdx !== 2 || (weights && Object.keys(weights).length > 0))` is always true: the route always supplies weights (query-fusion.ts:83 falls back to DEFAULT_WEIGHTS with 11 keys), so the `||` arm fires for every layer. The comment at intelligence-fusion-behaviors.ts:193 says balanced (41-60%) layers should be skipped 'to keep prompt concise', but with untouched default 50% sliders — the common case — ALL 11 balanced layers print when the design says ZERO should. The full '=== AI LAYER CONFIGURATION ===' block is ~1,700-1,900 chars ≈ 425-475 tokens, riding dynamicContext (route.ts:524-525) which is never prompt-cached (multi-provider-api.ts:80), so every non-cache-hit query pays full input rate; the intended concise form is ~100-150 tokens. Waste ≈ 300-450 tokens/query × ~100% of LLM-hitting traffic ≈ $0.002/query Opus — small per query, but it is the single largest fully-deterministic string re-sent uncached on every call.
findings.fix: Honor the original intent: change line 195 to skip tier-2 layers unless the user's weight actually deviates from 0.5 (e.g. `if (tierIdx !== 2 || Math.abs((weights?.[layerId] ?? 0.5) - 0.5) > 0.1)`). Optionally move the whole layer block into a second cache_control breakpoint since it is deterministic per (weights, methodology).

findings.title: Hidden per-query synopsis LLM call feeds up to 10 FULL query+answer pairs (5-20K tokens) to produce 2-3 sentences
findings.impact: generateSynopsis builds its prompt from up to 10 queries JOINed with their complete finalAnswer texts (side-canal.ts:229-251 — no truncation on result?.finalAnswer, which routinely runs 2-6K chars). It fires fire-and-forget on every query that maps to an existing topic (query-response-builder.ts:89-90). That is a 5-20K-token input call on MODELS.budget (Haiku 4.5, $1/M) to emit a 200-max-token synopsis — per query, invisible in the main pipeline, and the output then feeds the unbounded synopsis pileup in the previous finding. ~$0.005-0.02/query, comparable to the pageContext waste despite the cheap model.
findings.fix: Truncate each answer to ~400-500 chars in the context builder (side-canal.ts:246-251), cap at 5 Q+A pairs, and only regenerate the synopsis every Nth query on a topic (e.g. when query count since last synopsis >= 5) instead of every query.

findings.title: Retry × fallback stacking with no route deadline: worst case ~27 min vs platform kill mid-pipeline
findings.impact: Primary withRetry maxAttempts=3 (lines 799-841) retries 'timeout' errors, so one hung provider costs up to 3 × PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS (180s default, multi-provider-api.ts:16) ≈ 9 min; then the fallback chain tries up to 6 more providers (lines 851-877), each another 180s ≈ +18 min. sc-multipath is 3 samples × 2 attempts (lines 723-764). No route in app/ exports maxDuration (verified: zero grep hits), so Vercel kills the function at plan ceiling mid-work — the catch at line 897 that marks status=failed never runs on a platform kill, re-opening the stuck-pending window the c5e7f9a fix closed. Also lane stacking: a tot failure falls through to this full chain (lines 365-372) after already burning tot's advisor+synthesis budget. Mitigating: no client auto-retry exists (only manual retry buttons), so there's no third multiplier.
findings.fix: Cap retries of timeout-class errors at 1 (a 180s hang is not transient), lower PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS or make the per-attempt signal ~60s, cap the fallback chain at 2-3 providers, and export maxDuration on simple-query/tot-consensus so the platform ceiling is explicit and the failed-status write happens before it.

findings.title: Advisor fan-out can storm the shared 20 req/min OpenRouter free budget; 429 is itself a retry trigger
findings.impact: One consensus = 3 advisors × 2 rounds × (+1 auto-router retry each) = up to 12 calls on ONE shared key with a ~20 req/min free budget, and 429 is a fallback trigger (openrouter.ts:46-50) so rate-limiting begets more calls; openrouter.ts:6-8 and route.ts:42-43 explicitly acknowledge 'no throttling code needed yet'. Two concurrent tot queries exceed the budget, all advisors 429 → all-fail path → each falls through to simple-query's standard pipeline, which for the openrouter lane hits the same rate-limited key again (with 3 retries on 'rate' errors, simple-query:834).
findings.fix: Add a tiny in-process token-bucket (or p-limit) around postChat in lib/openrouter.ts, and make 429 fallback wait for the Retry-After header instead of retrying immediately.

findings.title: 4 parallel Brave news fetches with no timeout (headers or body)
findings.impact: GET /api/news fires 4 fetches with no signal (fetch line 106, res.json() line 117 both unbounded). Promise.allSettled never times out, so one stalled Brave socket hangs the route to the platform ceiling; with the 5-min cache empty (cold start), every dashboard visitor piles onto the hung path.
findings.fix: Add signal: AbortSignal.timeout(8000) to the fetch at line 106 (same as web-search-core.ts:71).

findings.title: Side Canal LLM calls (Anthropic line 26, OpenRouter line 63) have no timeout and are awaited in request paths
findings.impact: callLLM's two fetches have no signal — fetch and res.json() (lines 40, 81) unbounded. extractTopics/generateSynopsis are awaited in app/api/side-canal/extract/route.ts:41, app/api/side-canal/route.ts:72, and app/api/side-canal/synopsis/route.ts:28; a hung Anthropic socket also delays the OpenRouter fallback indefinitely (the fallback only fires after the first call resolves or throws).
findings.fix: Add AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) to both fetches in callLLM.

findings.title: buffers Map has no TTL — any query ending without a terminal event orphans its backlog permanently
findings.impact: Buffers are freed only 3s after a 'complete'/'error' emission (lines 237-242). Five paths in simple-query/route.ts return after the 'received' emit (line 106) creates a buffer but never emit a terminal: crypto early-return (line 270), daily-budget 402 (line 279), tot success (line 356), no-keys 500 (line 410), all-providers-failed 500 (line 910). Same for any uncaught crash that escapes the route's catch (e.g. process-level abort mid-stream). On the long-lived PM2 process (referenced at route.ts:614) these accumulate until restart — up to 600 events/~300KB per orphan, unbounded count.
findings.fix: Add a TTL sweep: stamp each buffer with a createdAt and run a periodic (e.g. 10-min) sweep deleting buffers older than the max query lifetime (~5 min), mirroring the stale-pending DB scavenger. Alternatively emit a terminal event on every return path (fixes the client too).

findings.title: no-keys and all-providers-failed paths emit no 'error' thought event — DB status was fixed (c5e7f9a) but the SSE terminal was not
findings.impact: Both failure returns (lines 410-415 and 910-917) mark the DB row 'failed' but never call emitAndPersist with stage:'error'. The live panel receives no terminal stage: the MetadataStrip stays in live/processing state until the fetch rejection lands, the client EventSource is never closed via the terminal path (compounding the catch/finally leak), and the server buffer orphans. This is the exact asymmetry c5e7f9a fixed for query status but missed for the thought stream.
findings.fix: Emit `{ stage: 'error', data: <short message> }` via emitAndPersist immediately before both `return NextResponse.json(..., { status: 500 })` calls — same pattern as the outer catch at lines 1126-1134.

findings.title: Post-cleanup grounding emit resurrects a deleted buffer that is never freed; live grounding delivery is dead in all cases
findings.impact: emitThought unconditionally re-creates buffers[queryId] (lines 209-215). scoreGroundingAsync fires AFTER the 'complete' emission (route.ts:1046) with an NLI fetch timeout of 8s (grounding-client.ts:61) — if the grounding event lands after the 3s cleanup (thought-stream.ts:238-242), it recreates a 1-event buffer with no terminal to ever free it: one permanent orphan per slow-guard query. Separately, the client closes its EventSource the instant 'complete' arrives (useQueryHandlers.ts:376), so even a fast grounding event is never delivered over SSE — the server's 3s grace window serves a subscriber that is guaranteed gone; the grounding stage only survives via the DB addEvent persistence.
findings.fix: In emitThought, drop (don't buffer) events for queryIds already terminated — keep a short-lived tombstone set of completed queryIds. For delivery, either emit grounding before 'complete', or have the client delay close until a 'grounding' event or a short timeout.

findings.title: No reconnect: onerror permanently closes the stream mid-query — and enabling native retry would duplicate the answer
findings.impact: onerror does evtSource.close() (lines 382-385), disabling EventSource's built-in auto-reconnect. A transient drop mid-query (proxy idle timeout during tot's silent 30s+ advisor waits — the SSE route sends no heartbeat/keepalive comments, route.ts:23-61) silences the live panel and the answer-stream fast path for the rest of the query; the answer still arrives via res.json(). Note the design coupling: if reconnect were allowed, the server replays the ENTIRE backlog on connect (thought-stream route.ts:34-37) and pushMetadata does no event-id dedup (side-canal-store.ts:104-118), so the 'complete'-time join of 'generating' narratives (useQueryHandlers.ts:358-364) would render duplicated answer text. The current no-reconnect choice is load-bearing.
findings.fix: Add event-id dedup in pushMetadata (skip if last event for messageId already has this id, or keep a Set of seen ids per message), then remove the close() in onerror to restore native reconnect; add a 15s `: ping` comment interval in the SSE route to survive idle proxies.

findings.title: Three deploy paths disagree on run mode: output:'standalone' vs `next start`
findings.impact: next.config.js:11 sets output:'standalone', but `start` is `next start -p 3000` (package.json:10), deploy.sh:47 PM2-starts `npx next start -p 3000`, quick-deploy.sh restarts that process, Dockerfile:79 runs `pnpm start`, and the standalone script's own ROLLBACK (line 41) uses `pm2 start npm -- start`. Next 15 warns `next start` does not work with standalone output; which server actually runs in prod depends on which script was used last, and rollback lands on the deprecated path.
findings.fix: Pick standalone as the single run mode: change `start` to `node .next/standalone/packages/web/server.js` (or delete the script), retire deploy.sh/quick-deploy.sh, and make rollback restart the previous standalone tree instead of `next start`.

findings.title: No PM2 ecosystem file — ad-hoc inline starts with no memory limit, no log rotation, inconsistent process names
findings.impact: No ecosystem.config.* exists anywhere in the repo (verified via find). PM2 processes are created inline (deploy.sh:47, quick-deploy-standalone.sh boot/cutover lines) with no --max-memory-restart on a small VPS (a leak becomes an OOM-kill loop), no log rotation, and two process names (akhai, akhai-next) whose state lives only in pm2 save on the dying host — none of it reproducible on the new box.
findings.fix: Commit deploy/ecosystem.config.cjs (name, cwd to the standalone tree, script server.js, env NODE_ENV/PORT/HOSTNAME, max_memory_restart ~600M, instances 1 — SQLite forbids clustering) and have all scripts use `pm2 startOrReload` against it; add pm2-logrotate to setup-vps.sh.

findings.title: Dockerfile is broken and stale: build fails env validation, ignores standalone output
findings.impact: lib/env.ts requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and OPENROUTER_API_KEY at build time, but the Dockerfile defines no ARG/ENV for them and doesn't set SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION, so `RUN pnpm build` (line 30) fails in any clean docker build; docker-compose.yml passes only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at runtime. The runner stage also predates output:'standalone' (copies whole .next, re-installs prod node_modules, runs `pnpm start`). docs/deployment/DEPLOYMENT_QUICKSTART.md builds this Dockerfile via Coolify from the wrong repo URL (github.com/algoq/akhai.git).
findings.fix: Either fix the Dockerfile for the standalone output (build with SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=1, copy .next/standalone + static + public, CMD node server.js) or delete Dockerfile/docker-compose.yml/Coolify docs so the PM2 path is unambiguously the only deploy story.

findings.title: Env var name mismatches: documented names are never read (Reown/WalletConnect, Brave)
findings.impact: Web3Provider.tsx:8 reads NEXT_PUBLIC_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID but .env.example:60 documents NEXT_PUBLIC_REOWN_PROJECT_ID — setting the documented var does nothing and the committed fallback project id ('87e52e87…') silently serves prod wallet auth. lib/realtime-data.ts:133 reads BRAVE_API_KEY while templates and the rest of the code use BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY, so searchWithBrave() always warns 'key not configured' and returns null in prod.
findings.fix: Rename the reads to the documented names (or vice versa): one canonical NEXT_PUBLIC_REOWN_PROJECT_ID and one BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY; drop the hardcoded WalletConnect fallback id.

findings.title: Sentry source-map upload permanently disabled behind a stale comment — prod stack traces stay minified
findings.impact: disableServerWebpackPlugin/disableClientWebpackPlugin (lines 95-96) were gated on 'until DSN is set', but Sentry went live in c5e7f9a; SENTRY_ORG/PROJECT/AUTH_TOKEN are documented in .env.example yet never used. Every prod error arrives unsymbolicated, which blunts the observability lane's value during launch triage.
findings.fix: Remove the two disable flags (upload runs only when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is present in the build env) and add SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN to the production build-machine env docs alongside the NEXT_PUBLIC build-time note.

findings.title: "Grounding Guard enabled for zero hallucinations" — an impossible guarantee the guard cannot make
findings.impact: The post-purchase page promises 'zero hallucinations'. The deployed guard (query-pipeline.ts:332-500) is a post-hoc keyword/regex heuristic that emits warnings and never blocks or verifies facts. The terms page itself says responses 'may contain errors, hallucinations' (terms/page.tsx:26) — the product contradicts itself at the exact moment a user has just paid. Ironically the guard's own impossibleClaims list flags '100% guaranteed' as 'Absolute certainty' (query-pipeline.ts:479).
findings.fix: Change to honest copy, e.g. 'Grounding Guard enabled — flags hype, drift, and unsupported claims'.

findings.title: Guard capability overclaims: 'Factuality' detector, claim verification, and 5% drift alerts don't match the shipped guard
findings.impact: Help page: '4 detectors (Hype, Echo, Drift, Factuality)'. README.md:217-224: 'Claim Verification: Cross-references factual statements', 'Source Attribution: Links claims to supporting evidence', 'alerts if > 5% drift'. The shipped web guard has hype word-lists, duplicate-sentence echo, word-overlap drift triggering at score > 80 (not 5%; query-pipeline.ts:427), and sanity regexes — no factuality detector runs: the core FactualityDetector (packages/core/src/grounding) is imported into web as types only, and the async grounding client is a lexical heuristic unless an optional GUARD_NLI_URL box is deployed (grounding-client.ts:14, honestly labeled 'parametric' with score null when there are no sources). The 'guard active' green dot is hardcoded on (ChatHeader.tsx:60-63; FunctionIndicators.tsx:127 default true).
findings.fix: Rewrite the help/README guard descriptions to match query-pipeline.ts + grounding-client.ts tiers (the internal code already uses honest labels — copy them outward), and drive the 'guard active' indicator from actual guard configuration/state.

findings.title: Third-party disclosure list omits Sentry, Resend, and crypto payment processors
findings.impact: The policy lists only Anthropic, OpenRouter, PostHog, Stripe, Brave Search. But: Sentry receives client+server error events (instrumentation-client.ts:10-38, instrumentation.ts:30-36 — well-scrubbed, but still an undisclosed processor); Resend receives user email addresses for magic-link auth (api/auth/email/route.ts:49-60); NOWPayments and BTCPay handle crypto payments offered on /pricing (lib/nowpayments.ts, lib/btcpay.ts). Line 16's 'wallet addresses or GitHub IDs for authentication' also omits email auth entirely.
findings.fix: Add Sentry (error monitoring), Resend (transactional email), NOWPayments/BTCPay (crypto payments) to the third-party section and mention email as an auth method.

findings.title: Published accuracy/hallucination numbers ('0.8% hallucination', '96.2% accuracy', 94-99% per methodology) have zero eval backing
findings.impact: docs/OPUS_4.5_ARCHITECTURE.md:700-701 states 'Hallucination Rate 0.8%' and 'Query Classification Accuracy 96.2% — A/B test vs ground truth'; docs/METHODOLOGIES_EXPLAINED.md:53,167,443,758 claims per-methodology accuracy up to 99% and '<1% hallucination rate'. The repo's only benchmark is bench/guard_bench.py — a RAM/latency check with 12 canned samples (6 fabricated answers), not an accuracy eval. No dataset, harness, or results artifact exists anywhere. If the repo ships public under Apache 2.0 (README.md:338), these are fabricated metrics published under the honesty brand.
findings.fix: Delete the numbers or replace with 'target' language until a real eval exists; if keeping any figure, commit the eval harness and dataset that produced it.

findings.title: Dead 277-line core CoD module with fabricated 'measured' savings metadata
findings.impact: Zero runtime consumers in web/api/mcp-server (verified by grep; web builds its own prompt). Its metadata is fake-computed: estimatedCoTTokens = totalTokens * 12.5 makes savingsPercent identically 92 by arithmetic construction (lines 137-138), tokenSavings is the literal string '~92% vs CoT' (line 155), and it includes input tokens in the multiplier though CoD savings only apply to output. Also `(provider as any).family` (line 130) violates the project's no-any rule, and extractFinalAnswer (line 204) matches the FIRST 'So:/Thus:/Therefore:' in the text — a mid-reasoning step would be extracted over the real final answer.
findings.fix: Delete the module (and registry.ts execute wiring) or wire it as the actual cod path; if kept, compute savings from a real CoT baseline, not a constant.

findings.title: Three inconsistent user-facing token/cost figures for cod across UI components
findings.impact: methodology-data.ts:56-58 says '~400' tokens/$0.012, MethodologyExplorer.tsx metrics say '600-1000'/$0.030, MethodologyFrame.tsx:54-56 says '~600'/$0.012 — mutually inconsistent, and all far below realistic triple-draft output under a 2000-token ceiling. Cosmetic credibility issue on top of the HIGH claim finding.
findings.fix: Single-source cod metrics from one module and set numbers from observed production output.

findings.title: pas system prompt self-contradicts: 'no summaries, no bullets' then mandates a 5-sentence summary and 5 labeled sections
findings.impact: writingStyle (query-pipeline.ts:286) commands 'No bullet lists...', 'No summary of what you just said', 'No To summarize' — then synthesisSection (line 293) requires a '## SYNTHESIS' of exactly 5 summary sentences ('rapid overview of your entire analysis') and the pas block (line 317) mandates [UNDERSTAND][PLAN][EXECUTE][VERIFY][ANSWER] structure plus [RELATED]/[NEXT] (line 290). ~500 tokens of style rules are immediately overridden by format rules in the same prompt; output quality depends on which instruction the model weights.
findings.fix: Scope the brevity rules ('outside the mandated sections') or trim the contradicting lines from writingStyle for structured methodologies.

findings.title: Fabricated metrics on pas UI cards: '+24% savings' and token/latency numbers nothing measures
findings.impact: methodology-data.ts:84-89 shows pas with savings '+24%' — that figure is the PoT paper's accuracy delta (pas.ts:10) repurposed as 'savings' for a prompt-only PaS implementation; MethodologyExplorer.tsx pas metrics claim 'tokens: 400-600' while the actual output budget is 3000 max tokens (output-budgets.ts:8). No telemetry backs any of these numbers.
findings.fix: Replace with measured values from the COGS scorecard or drop the metrics row from the pas card.

findings.title: Core prompt self-contradicts and the parser discards multi-line output (relevant only if core path is revived)
findings.impact: System prompt demands 'respond with EXACTLY ONE of' (react.ts:358) but the example at 370-375 shows a 6-line trajectory including an 'Observation:' line the model must never emit — inviting hallucinated observations and multi-line replies. All three parse regexes are ^-anchored, non-multiline (react.ts:390/399/420): a 'Thought:\n...Action:' reply keeps only the thought's first line and discards the action, wasting a full iteration; unparsable output defaults to 'thought' (react.ts:428-432), so a model that just answers plainly loops all 10 iterations and returns 'Unable to determine answer' (react.ts:332) after generating the answer 10 times. Param regex (\w+)="([^"]+)" (react.ts:406) truncates finish answers at embedded quotes; \((.+?)\) (react.ts:399) breaks on ')' or newlines. Context grows unbounded and is fully re-sent each iteration (react.ts:171/220/250/274/290) — quadratic token cost; provider.complete at react.ts:197 is unguarded, so one mid-loop provider failure discards all prior work.
findings.fix: Moot if the dead-code finding above is resolved by deletion; otherwise rewrite parser as multiline scan, fix the example to a single-step response, and cap/summarize context.

findings.title: 'Web search' label and stale 'Tools: search, calc' tooltip overstate non-live react runs
findings.impact: METHOD_PLAIN.react = 'Web search', and methodology-data.ts:225-231 promises 'real web search' — true only for signed-in users with REACT_AGENT_LIVE≠0 (route.ts:618 requires userId). Anonymous and flag-off runs execute the knowledge-recall prompt (query-pipeline.ts, case 'react') where only the generic 3-result auto-search context is live, yet the UI label is identical. Separately, methodology-data.ts:74 tooltip says 'Tools: search, calc' — the live agent has only web_search (react-agent.ts:91); the calculator exists solely in the dead core code.
findings.fix: Differentiate the label (e.g. 'Knowledge recall' when the agent path is skipped) or note the degradation in the stream event; update the tooltip to 'Tool: web search'.

findings.title: React branch ignores provider selection and Anthropic key availability
findings.impact: The condition checks only method id, flag, and userId. If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unset, getProviderForMethodology already selected openrouter (provider-selector.ts:66-68, 96), but the branch still calls runReactAgent, which defaults to anthropic(MODELS.premium) (react-agent.ts:50) — guaranteed failure, then the full fallback chain runs. Every signed-in react query in that misconfiguration pays the failure detour. Also stale comment: line 619 says 'feature-gated, default OFF. Flag-off keeps prod byte-identical' directly under the E5 header (611) saying the agent 'is now the DEFAULT'.
findings.fix: Add selectedProvider === 'anthropic' (or an env key check) to the branch condition; delete the stale line-619 comment.

findings.title: SC_MULTIPATH mode drops live answer streaming (regression vs the just-shipped answer-stream UX)
findings.impact: Multipath samples pass no onTextDelta/onThinkingDelta (route.ts:725-734) while the default path streams (route.ts:810-811). With SC_MULTIPATH=1, sc queries lose the instant token streaming shipped in commit 4ae60fd — user stares at 'Draft i of n' events until all samples complete. Also cosmetic: 'Draft 2 of 3 / 3 of 3' progress fires synchronously before the parallel samples launch (sc-multipath.ts:163-166), so progress does not track completion. LOW only because the flag defaults OFF.
findings.fix: Stream the first (cache-writing) sample's text deltas to the client, reconcile if a different sample is picked central; or emit per-sample completion (not launch) progress.

findings.title: SC_SAMPLE_TEMPERATURE (and single-pass temperature 0.7) is inert for Anthropic — the primary provider
findings.impact: callAnthropic never maps request.temperature into the body (multi-provider-api.ts:284-294 builds body without a temperature field), acknowledged in sc-multipath.ts:16-17. All Anthropic sc calls run at API default temp; sample diversity in multipath relies on default randomness, and the 0.9 knob only affects non-Anthropic fallbacks. Behavior is acceptable but the knob is illusory and the code reads as if diversity were controlled.
findings.fix: Either pass temperature through in callAnthropic (when extendedThinking is off) or delete the constant and route param and document reliance on default-temp sampling.

findings.title: Within core sc.ts: BoT's headline mechanism is unreachable and the refinement call references context that is never provided
findings.impact: ThoughtBuffer is per-invocation (sc.ts:226) so meta-buffers are always empty at Stage 1 (sc.ts:239-241); distillation needs >=10 thoughts (sc.ts:106-108) but the prompt asks for 3-5 steps (sc.ts:402) → Stage 2 (sc.ts:269-274) effectively never fires → 'Reuses distilled knowledge across similar problems' (sc.ts:11) is false. The refinement prompt orders 'Refine your analysis... using insights from previous reasoning' and 'Validate against meta-buffer patterns' (sc.ts:411-417) yet the request contains only that single message (sc.ts:283-287) — no prior reasoning attached, so call 2 of 3 is an independent re-sample, pure duplicate spend (3 sequential calls, up to 800 output tokens each). Latent crashes if distillation ever fired: reduce on empty array throws and Math.max(...[]) = -Infinity (sc.ts:348-349); seed thought 'Analyzing problem: ...' at conf 0.9 (sc.ts:230-235) wastes a synthesis slot (filter >=0.7, sc.ts:518). All LOW solely because the file is dead at runtime (see MED finding).
findings.fix: If the file is kept: persist ThoughtBuffer across calls or lower maxBufferSize, include the initial completion in the refinement request messages, guard the empty-buffer math, and exclude the seed thought from synthesis.

findings.title: Dead METHOD_PLAIN keys 'bot' and 'pot' — ids that cannot occur
findings.impact: The web Methodology union is 'direct'|'cod'|'sc'|'react'|'pas'|'tot'|'auto' (query-classifier.ts:17); 'bot' (:161) and 'pot' (:163) are unreachable map entries left over from the sc/bot and pas/pot renames — live evidence of the id drift behind the HIGH naming finding.
findings.fix: Delete the two dead keys.

findings.title: Stale docs: 'GTP: 4 AIs debate' and 'DeepSeek R1, Mistral Large 2, xAI Grok 3 = GTP consensus advisors'
findings.impact: Actual advisors are 3 free OpenRouter models — openai/gpt-oss-120b:free, cohere/north-mini-code:free, meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free (packages/web/lib/models.ts:16-21) — with Anthropic synthesis; there are 3 advisors, not 4, and no DeepSeek/Mistral/xAI involvement in tot. Anyone (human or agent) following CLAUDE.md will reason from a false architecture.
findings.fix: Update CLAUDE.md's Key APIs and methodology table to the free-OpenRouter 3-advisor + Anthropic-synthesizer reality.

findings.title: Tier-2 'skip balanced layers' condition is dead — all 11 layers always emitted
findings.impact: if (tierIdx !== 2 || (weights && Object.keys(weights).length > 0)) — runFusionPipeline always passes non-empty weights (client's or the 11-key DEFAULT_WEIGHTS, query-fusion.ts:83), so the condition is always true. The comment's promise ('skip 41-60% range to keep prompt concise') never happens: ~1.3KB of layer instructions ride the UNCACHED dynamicContext on every standard/sc call, paying input tokens even for all-default configs where BALANCED text adds no signal.
findings.fix: Decide intent: either drop the dead clause and always emit (delete misleading comment), or skip tier-2 layers whose weight equals the 0.5 default.

findings.title: Math.random() base activation makes dominance and auto-methodology nondeterministic
findings.impact: activation = 0.15 + Math.random() * 0.1 for keyword-less layers; with weight 1.0 the effective weight lands in [0.15, 0.25] straddling the >0.15 dominance threshold, so dominantLayers — and the 0.2-0.3 methodology score adders keyed on them — flip randomly per request. This contradicts route.ts:213-214's claim that 'fusion has resolved it deterministically' for the R2 cache re-check (consequence is safe misses, not wrong hits). It also jitters the emitted 'layers' UI event, where query-fusion.ts:271 additionally reports effectiveWeight AS the user's 'weight' (a 100% slider can display as 15-25%).
findings.fix: Use a deterministic base (e.g. flat 0.15 or a query-hash), and report weight and activation as separate fields in the layers event.

findings.title: Round-2 'Other advisors said' context includes the recipient's own round-1 answer
findings.impact: round1Context (lines 298-300) is built from ALL successful round-1 responses and injected identically into every advisor's round-2 system prompt (line 307), so each advisor is told its own answer came from 'other advisors' — mild self-reinforcement bias and wasted context tokens in a 500-char-per-advisor budget.
findings.fix: Build round1Context per-recipient, filtering out r.provider === provider inside the round2Promises map.

findings.title: Both UI consumers of consensus/confidence numbers are dead code with unreachable color thresholds
findings.impact: GTPConsensusView is imported nowhere (grep across app/ and components/ finds only self-references); VerificationWindow (the only FlashProgress host, VerificationWindow.tsx:136) is likewise unimported. So totData.finalConsensus and per-advisor confidence currently reach no user. If revived as-is: the emerald '>=0.85' round badge (line 252) is unreachable (max 0.833), so rounds would render permanently red/yellow at '50%', and finalConsensus renders in emerald text (lines 96-99) regardless of value.
findings.fix: Delete GTPConsensusView.tsx, VerificationWindow.tsx, and FlashProgress.tsx (or wire them up deliberately after fixing the metric); until then they are misleading dashboards waiting to be mounted.

findings.title: README stale: describes SSE event-shape calibration for a provider that does plain JSON POST
findings.impact: README says 'the SSE provider documents its event-shape assumption... adjust stage/field names' but akhai-sse.js:1-4 states it was verified 2026-07 as a single JSON object, not SSE. Misdirects the next person calibrating a failure. Filename akhai-sse.js is also misnamed.
findings.fix: Rewrite README run/calibration section to match the JSON provider; optionally rename to akhai-simple-query.js (its id() already says that).

findings.title: Latency assert (60s) and provider abort (90s) disagree
findings.impact: A 61-89s response burns the full wait then fails on latency; a >90s response surfaces as a provider error rather than a latency failure — two different failure signatures for the same underlying slowness, muddying triage.
findings.fix: Align the two (e.g. provider timeout 65s) so slow == latency-fail consistently.

findings.title: No rate limiting on email verification-code 'send' action
findings.impact: POST /api/auth/email action='send' generates a code and calls Resend for any submitted email with no throttling. An attacker can bomb arbitrary inboxes with AkhAI codes and burn the Resend quota. Zod validation is present (AuthEmailSchema:15) but does not limit request rate.
findings.fix: Add per-email / per-IP rate limiting (e.g. max N sends per 10min window) before generating and sending the code.

findings.title: OpenRouter fallback uses PAID llama slug — missing ':free' suffix
findings.impact: Fallback model is 'meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct' while lib/models.ts free tier is 'meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free' — when both Anthropic models fail, the 'free' fallback silently bills OpenRouter credits.
findings.fix: Append ':free' (or reference MODELS.free) in the MODELS array entry.

findings.title: Unauthenticated GET proxies NOWPayments payment-status and min-amount lookups
findings.impact: Anyone can poll payment status for guessable/leaked paymentIds and burn NOWPayments API quota via unlimited currency min-amount lookups; no rate limit, no session.
findings.fix: Require session for status checks (or verify the paymentId belongs to the session user via the orderId encoding); cache min-amount responses server-side.

findings.title: limit/offset parseInt without NaN guard
findings.impact: ?limit=abc → NaN → better-sqlite3 bind failure → 500 instead of 400. Also GET query params are outside the zod ratchet here (no schema).
findings.fix: Use z.coerce.number().int().min(0) defaults for limit/offset, or Number.isFinite fallback.

findings.title: Unauthenticated health endpoint discloses API-key presence and query count
findings.impact: Anonymous callers learn which provider keys are configured (lines 20-22) and total query volume (lines 13-14) — mild recon value.
findings.fix: Return only status/latency publicly; move checks detail behind an admin token or strip key names.

findings.title: Open unthrottled proxy to Nominatim risks OSM IP ban
findings.impact: Nominatim's usage policy caps at ~1 req/s; an unauthenticated, unratelimited proxy lets any client burn the server's quota and get the deployment IP blocked, breaking the feature for everyone.
findings.fix: Add a small in-memory rate limiter / cache keyed by city; endpoint is otherwise clean (zod at line 6).

findings.title: limit query param unbounded and NaN-unsafe
findings.impact: parseInt with no cap or NaN guard: ?limit=-1 makes SQLite return every row for the conversation (LIMIT -1 = unlimited); ?limit=abc yields NaN which better-sqlite3 rejects, producing a 500 with error details leaked in the response body (:221).
findings.fix: Clamp like the sibling route: Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(...)||50,1),100) — mindmap/topics/[id]/queries/route.ts:46 already does the cap half.

findings.title: offset param NaN-unsafe (limit is correctly capped, offset is not guarded)
findings.impact: ?offset=abc → parseInt → NaN → better-sqlite3 bind error → 500. Cosmetic robustness gap; limit at :46 shows the correct pattern one line above.
findings.fix: const offset = Math.max(parseInt(searchParams.get('offset') || '0') || 0, 0).

findings.title: ExtractSchema.response has no max length
findings.impact: z.string().min(1) with no .max() — unbounded request body accepted (root route caps the same field at 60000, route-schemas.ts:206). Only substring(0,500) reaches the prompt, but extractKeywords runs over the full text: memory/CPU exposure on an unauthenticated endpoint.
findings.fix: Add .max(60000) to match SideCanalSchema.

findings.title: legendMode parsed but never used
findings.impact: ExtractSchema accepts legendMode (:18) and the handler destructures it (:38), but extractTopics is called without it (:41) — the extractTopics legendMode parameter (lib/side-canal.ts:123) is dead; clients toggling legend mode silently get default behavior.
findings.fix: Pass legendMode through: extractTopics(query, response, userId, legendMode) — or remove the field from the schema.

findings.title: GET handler performs a DB write
findings.impact: UPDATE user_points inside GET (:101-109) makes the endpoint non-idempotent; harmless today (own-row level recompute) but violates HTTP semantics and can surprise caching layers.
findings.fix: Move the level recompute to the point-award write path, or tolerate it as INFO.

findings.title: QuerySchema satisfies the zod ratchet nominally but has z.any() escapes and unconstrained history roles
findings.impact: pageContext (line 12), instinctConfig (line 26), grimoireContext (line 28) are z.any(); conversationHistory role is bare z.string() (line 11) later cast to the provider role union (route.ts:460-462), so a client can inject role:'system' turns or oversized unbounded objects into the prompt path. Not a crash vector (providers reject bad roles) but weakens the validated-input guarantee the ratchet claims.
findings.fix: role: z.enum(['user','assistant']); give pageContext/grimoireContext z.string().max(N) (they are interpolated as text); type instinctConfig to its real shape; cap conversationHistory length like ChatMessagesSchema does (route-schemas.ts:106-108).

findings.title: Unvalidated ?limit param — NaN throws a 500, negative disables the LIMIT
findings.impact: parseInt(searchParams.get('limit') || '50') binds directly into `LIMIT ?` (line 83-84). ?limit=abc → NaN → better-sqlite3 binding error → 500 (caught, but noisy). ?limit=-1 → SQLite treats as no limit → full scan/serialization of every gnostic row the user owns.
findings.fix: Clamp: const limit = Math.min(Math.max(Number(searchParams.get('limit')) || 50, 1), 200); or run params through a small zod schema like the POST routes.

findings.title: presets reads wrong cookie name (akhai_session vs akhai_session_id)
findings.impact: The route reads cookie `akhai_session` (lines 15, 82, 123) but session-manager.ts sets `akhai_session_id` (lines 39, 66). sessionId is therefore likely always null, so POST/DELETE always 401 and GET never returns user presets — the endpoint is effectively inert for saved presets. Functional, not a security hole, but ships broken.
findings.fix: Align on one cookie name (akhai_session_id) across the route and session-manager.

findings.title: Unvalidated videoId interpolated into fixed third-party hosts
findings.impact: see report §5.B2
findings.fix: shared assertPublicUrl guard

findings.title: updateQuery() interpolates Object.keys(updates) into SET clause without runtime whitelist
findings.impact: Not exploitable today: all six callers (app/api/simple-query/route.ts:260,325,404,898,1006,1145) pass object literals with hard-coded keys, and the TypeScript signature constrains keys at compile time. But the guarantee is compile-time only — if a future caller ever spreads request JSON into the updates object (e.g. updateQuery(id, {...body}, userId)), attacker-controlled keys are interpolated raw into SQL (`${f} = ?`), yielding SET-clause injection on the queries table.
findings.fix: Add a runtime whitelist before building the SET clause: const ALLOWED = new Set(['status','result','tokens_used','cost','gnostic_metadata','raw_thinking']); const fields = Object.keys(updates).filter(f => ALLOWED.has(f)); (or throw on unknown keys). One-line hardening, zero behavior change.

findings.title: Pre-rewrite backup refs (refs/original/*) still retain the purged .specstory key locally
findings.impact: The filter-branch that removed .specstory left refs/original/refs/heads/main, refs/original/refs/heads/crypto-payments-v1, and refs/original/refs/stash in this clone (verified via merge-base --is-ancestor 7473d58) — the sk-ant-api03-AF6… key stays recoverable from any copy of this working clone (212M .git), including backups of the developer machine.
findings.fix: git for-each-ref --format='%(refname)' refs/original | xargs -n1 git update-ref -d; then git reflog expire --expire=now --all && git gc --prune=now. Purely local cleanup; do after key rotation.

findings.title: Inconsistent session-token entropy across providers (randomUUID vs randomBytes(32))
findings.impact: Primary path createSession() uses randomBytes(32).toString('hex') = 256-bit tokens (lib/db/auth.ts:140). X and email routes instead use generateUUID()=crypto.randomUUID() = 122-bit v4 (x/callback:91, email/route.ts:141), and dev-login uses randomBytes(24)=192-bit. All are CSPRNG-backed and 122 bits is not brute-forceable, so this is not itself a vulnerability — but the inconsistency means one code path could regress unnoticed and reviewers can't assume a single token contract.
findings.fix: Standardize all session-token minting on createSession()/randomBytes(32) rather than duplicating ad-hoc INSERTs into the sessions table in each OAuth route.

findings.title: Dead duplicate modules each carrying their own cache: ddg-search.ts and app/api/query/rate-limit.ts imported nowhere
findings.impact: ddg-search.ts duplicates the entire searchWeb + ddgCache + backoff implementation living in enhanced-links/route.ts:182-260; app/api/query/rate-limit.ts duplicates lib/rate-limit.ts with different semantics (token bucket vs fixed window). Neither is imported by any file (verified via grep). Zero runtime cost, but a future fix applied to the dead copy silently no-ops — exactly the drift class the c497ce6 hygiene commit was purging.
findings.fix: Delete both files, or extract the live enhanced-links searchWeb into ddg-search.ts and import it so one copy exists.

findings.title: 10-minute max-timeout is never cleared in cleanup(), pinning each closed SSE connection's closure for up to 10 min
findings.impact: cleanup() (lines 65-84) clears the heartbeat and unsubscribes, but the 600000ms setTimeout at line 153 is not tracked or cleared, so every stream — including ones that complete in 2s — retains its controller/sendEvent closure (and the captured queryData reference) until the timer fires. Bounded by connection rate x 10 min window, so a slow ballast rather than a leak; also each fired timer on an already-closed stream is a no-op thanks to the isClosed guard.
findings.fix: Store the timeout id and clearTimeout it inside cleanup(), mirroring heartbeatInterval handling.

findings.title: queries Map cap is entry-count-based (500), not byte-based — worst-case footprint is large but finite
findings.impact: Each QueryData holds the full result object plus an uncapped events array (addQueryEvent pushes unconditionally, line 128); TOT queries carry complete advisor responses per event. 500 heavyweight entries could plausibly hold tens of MB. Acceptable for launch on a single node — flagged so the number is a conscious choice, not an accident. Restart is safe: SQLite is the source of truth and loadQuery (line 151) backfills.
findings.fix: None required pre-launch; if memory pressure appears, cap events per entry or shrink MAX_CACHED_QUERIES.

findings.title: conversationHistory message content and pageContext are unbounded at the schema boundary
findings.impact: QuerySchema caps query at 10K chars but conversationHistory items are z.string() with no max (schema.ts:11) and pageContext is z.any() (schema.ts:12). slice(-6) bounds message COUNT (route.ts:460) but not size — six 20K-char messages pass validation and become ~30K input tokens. A hostile or buggy client can inflate any query's cost arbitrarily; the daily token budget (route.ts:277) only catches it after the fact via output accounting.
findings.fix: Add .max(8000) to history item content and z.string().max(8000) for pageContext in QuerySchema; optionally truncate server-side per message before building messages.

findings.title: Secondary Anthropic routes without timeouts: guard-suggestions:81, idea-factory/generate:20, test-key:55/107
findings.impact: Each hangs its route on a stalled socket until platform kill. Low traffic, user-facing but non-critical surfaces; same one-line fix each.
findings.fix: Add signal: AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) to each fetch.

findings.title: Timeout mid-SSE-stream returns silently truncated answer recorded as outcome 'ok'
findings.impact: parseAnthropicStream catches the 180s abort (and any mid-body error) at lines 224-227 and returns whatever text accumulated; callProvider then records outcome 'ok' (line 147) and simple-query saves it as a complete answer. A long streamed answer that hits PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS is delivered cut off mid-sentence with no truncation marker, no COGS distinction, and no retry (content is non-empty so the PROVIDER_NO_CONTENT re-roll never fires).
findings.fix: Set a truncated flag on the response when the read loop exits via catch, surface it in recordCall outcome (e.g. 'truncated') and optionally append an honest '[answer truncated]' marker.

findings.title: Dead-but-hazardous modules: realtime-data.ts (3 no-timeout fetches, zero importers) and resilient-provider.ts (unused, doc claims a timeout it doesn't enforce)
findings.impact: realtime-data.ts fetches CoinGecko (line 47), CoinMarketCap (line 94), Brave (line 141) with no signal — no production callers (the live crypto path is query-pipeline.ts:136-138, which correctly uses AbortSignal.timeout(10_000)); it's a trap for future reuse and duplicates the symbol map. resilient-provider.ts callProviderSafe has zero callers and its '60s timeout per attempt' doc is false: execCtx.aborted is only checked BEFORE each attempt (line 51) and never aborts the in-flight fetch (real ceiling is callProvider's 180s). query-classifier.ts:235 classifyQueryWithAI (no timeout) is likewise test-only.
findings.fix: Delete lib/realtime-data.ts and lib/resilient-provider.ts (or add timeouts + a real per-attempt abort and wire them in); if kept, fix the resilient-provider doc-comment.

findings.title: Extended-thinking deltas are emitted per-delta uncoalesced — can exceed MAX_BUFFERED=600 and evict early stages from the replay backlog
findings.impact: thinkingCb (lines 558-578) emits one ThoughtEvent per raw thinking_delta (multi-provider-api.ts:209-212, no coalescing, unlike the answer's 40-char/250ms flushGen at 588-601). With budget_tokens=10000 (multi-provider-api.ts:300), a full trace (~40K chars) yields well over 600 events, so buffer.shift() (thought-stream.ts:215) evicts received/routing/layers — exactly the events the backlog exists to preserve (comment at 183-190). tot itself stays under the cap: ~15 stage events + ≤3 advisor fallbacks + ≤6 advisor/miss + round2-skip + ~50-400 gen-syn flushes (synthesis-stream.ts:94-107, 40-char coalescing, 4096-token cap) ≈ 60-420. Practical impact is small today because the client subscribes before the POST, so replay only covers the connect race.
findings.fix: Reuse the flushGen coalescing pattern for thinking deltas (40 chars/250ms), which also cuts SSE event volume ~10x; or make the buffer eviction stage-aware (never evict non-generating/non-reasoning-delta events).

findings.title: cancel() only cleans up the last subscriber — a disconnecting controller among 2+ is never removed
findings.impact: cancel() deletes the whole connections entry when size<=1 but does nothing otherwise (lines 52-60) — it never removes the specific cancelled controller. With two subscribers, the first disconnect leaves a dead controller pruned only lazily by an enqueue failure in emitThought (thought-stream.ts:222-233); if the query already ended without a terminal event, the connections entry (with dead controllers) persists forever. Also the backlog-replay enqueues in start() (lines 35-37) are not try/caught — a client that disconnects during replay throws inside start().
findings.fix: Capture the controller in a closure variable in start(), and in cancel() do controllers.delete(thisController) then delete the map entry if empty; wrap replay enqueues in try/catch.

findings.title: Node version unpinned across build machine, VPS, and native module ABI
findings.impact: setup-vps.sh installs Node 20; engines is only >=20; no .nvmrc; local dev/SHIELD runs Node 24 (better-sqlite3 ABI already bit this project per session memory). The standalone flow ships a linux better_sqlite3.node whose ABI must match the VPS Node — provision the new box with a different major and the app crashes at boot with ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED.
findings.fix: Pin one Node major everywhere: .nvmrc + exact engines range + same NodeSource major in setup-vps.sh, and make the deploy script verify `node -p process.versions.modules` on the VPS matches the binding it ships.

findings.title: DB backup cron is not provisioned — backups silently absent on the new host
findings.impact: backup-db.sh expects a manual `crontab -e` install (header, line 4) with hardcoded /home/akhai/app paths; setup-vps.sh installs no cron. After migration the SQLite DB (sole data store, WAL mode) has zero backups until someone remembers the manual step, and the standalone tree's data/ symlink indirection makes the hardcoded path easy to get wrong.
findings.fix: Install the cron line in setup-vps.sh (or a systemd timer), parameterize DB_PATH, and add off-box copy (rsync/rclone) since on-box backups die with the host.

findings.title: Support/community links are placeholders or inconsistent across surfaces
findings.impact: Help page points to docs.akhai.ai (line 33), discord.gg/akhai (85), and github.com/akhai (96) — the actual repo is github.com/algoq369/akhai (README.md:247). Pricing footer uses support@akhai.ai (pricing/page.tsx:404) while the privacy contact is algoq2039@proton.me (privacy/page.tsx:36) and the email sender domain is akhai.app (auth/email/route.ts:60). Users who need help will likely hit dead ends; help page also promises 'Within 24 hours' response time (line 70).
findings.fix: Verify each link/inbox exists before launch; standardize one support address and one canonical domain (akhai.app vs akhai.ai) everywhere.

findings.title: README claims stale/false stack and a pricing table that matches nothing in the app
findings.impact: README claims 'Neo4j (knowledge graph)' — zero neo4j references exist in any package; 'Next.js 14' vs actual Next 15; the pricing table (README.md:281-298) advertises Expert $100 and Legend $200 tiers plus pay-as-you-go rates ($15/M) that do not exist in pricing-config.ts (free/pro/instinct/team; credits at $50/M down to $25/M). Free tier '50K tokens' happens to match token-budget.ts but contradicts the pricing page's '10 queries per day'. Three public surfaces (README, /pricing, enforcement) describe three different products.
findings.fix: Rewrite the README stack and pricing sections from the actual code (pricing-config.ts + token-budget.ts) and add a drift check, mirroring the SHIELD pattern already used for model rates.

findings.title: Web-side pas error path is sound; core-side pas.ts loses all state on provider failure
findings.impact: Web: pas runs through withRetry (3 attempts, transient-only, route.ts:799-841), then the full 7-provider fallback chain (route.ts:851-887), then failed-status DB write + Sentry + 500 (route.ts:897-917) — no hang, no pending-row leak. Core pas.ts:195 has no try/catch around provider.complete: one mid-loop provider failure throws away all accumulated steps and token spend with no partial result — irrelevant while the function is dead code.
findings.fix: None needed web-side. If core pas is ever wired, wrap the completion call and return partial steps with executionSucceeded=false.

findings.title: Routing prompt conflates SC and BoT in one line and overlaps sc/tot examples
findings.impact: The AI-classifier prompt describes sc as 'Self-Consistency, buffers key facts and self-validates' — SC's name with BoT's mechanism — and its examples ('compare X and Y', 'evaluate Z') overlap tot's ('which is better X or Y'), inviting routing ambiguity between a 1-call and a multi-provider methodology with ~5x cost difference.
findings.fix: Rewrite the sc line to describe the actual buffered-validation behavior and pick non-overlapping examples.

findings.title: extractConsensus slice can include SYNTHESIS/[RELATED boilerplate, mildly inflating measured agreement
findings.impact: For header-style '[ANSWER]\ntext', the close-bracket cut lands on the ']' of the later '[RELATED]' marker, so the agreement string includes the ## SYNTHESIS section and the literal 'RELATED' token; the 30%-tail fallback likewise captures shared boilerplate. Common tokens present in every sample nudge Jaccard agreement upward. Small effect; consistency is display-only.
findings.fix: Trim at the '## SYNTHESIS' / '[RELATED' markers before measuring agreement.

findings.title: antipatternSuppression and processingMode store fields never reach any backend query
findings.impact: setAntipatternSuppression has zero call sites outside the store definition; the /api/simple-query body (useQueryHandlers.ts:400-426) sends neither antipatternSuppression nor processingMode — fusion computes processingMode itself from complexity (intelligence-fusion.ts:110-112). Dead persisted state.
findings.fix: Remove the dead fields or wire them into the query payload when the features ship.

findings.title: Strongest-round selection is sound for its observed failure mode; residual semantics quirks only
findings.impact: Selection (lines 359-363) picks the round with the most status==='complete' responses, ties going to the later round (>=) — correctly protects a 3/3 round 1 from a timeout-degraded 1/3 round 2, and correctly prefers refined round-2 answers on a 3/3 tie. consensusLevel plays no role in selection (fine, given it is noise). One-advisor rounds are currently impossible: all three advisors share OPENROUTER_API_KEY (lines 191-195) so availableProviders is 3-or-0, and round 2 only runs after a full round 1, so it always has 3 participants. Residual quirk: when only 1/3 completes round 1 (round 2 skipped), finalConsensus = 0 (calculateConsensus returns 0 for <2 successes, consensus-scoring.ts:50) atop a real synthesized answer — 'insufficient data' and 'zero agreement' are conflated in one number; metrics.successRate (line 484) carries the honest signal.
findings.fix: If finalConsensus ever becomes user-facing, emit null (not 0) when fewer than 2 advisors completed, so the UI can say 'n/a' instead of '0% consensus'.

findings.title: Einstein floor pass-list is broad enough to pass hedged fabrications
findings.impact: Any output containing 'cannot' or 'wasn' passes — 'I cannot be certain, but Einstein reportedly said...' followed by a fabricated quote would pass the floor.
findings.fix: Add a negative assert: output must NOT contain a quoted attribution (regex for quote marks adjacent to 'Einstein said').

findings.title: unvalidated_routes ratchet (0) satisfied by token match, not real zod validation
findings.impact: arboreal-chat validates its POST body with a hand-rolled parseBody() (line 12), not a zod schema. shield.sh's unvalidated_routes check greps each request.json() file for '.parse(|.safeParse(|Schema|z.object|parsed.'; this route passes solely because it uses the identifier 'parsed.'. The ratchet therefore does not actually guarantee zod coverage on this route.
findings.fix: Replace parseBody with a zod schema in lib/route-schemas (as auth/email does), or tighten the shield grep so 'parsed.' alone does not satisfy it.

findings.title: Runtime DDL (ALTER TABLE users) executed on every OAuth callback
findings.impact: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN x_username/x_id run inside try/catch on each callback (route.ts:75-79). Works but is a schema-migration-at-request-time smell; masks real errors and adds per-request overhead.
findings.fix: Move these columns into the schema/migration bootstrap and drop the per-request ALTERs.

findings.title: Ownership scoping done right — pattern worth replicating
findings.impact: Owned rows 404 for non-owners (same status as missing rows, no id-validity oracle); anonymous legacy rows deliberately public. This is the exact ownership pattern depth-extract (HIGH above) is missing.
findings.fix: No change needed; reuse this pattern in depth-extract.

findings.title: Council advertises multi-AI consensus but routes all 5 agents to Anthropic in prod
findings.impact: The 'Direct env check' at lines 23-25 sends every agent to anthropic whenever ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set (i.e., always in prod), so agent.provider diversity is dead code — product claim vs. behavior drift.
findings.fix: If intentional, document it; otherwise prefer agent.provider when that provider's key exists and only fall back per-agent.

findings.title: Hardcoded model id bypasses MODELS single-source (dev-only route)
findings.impact: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514' is inlined instead of using lib/models MODELS — drift risk against the rate-table guard from commit c497ce6. Cost exposure is nil in prod because the route 404s (route.ts:38), and configs are capped at 20 by LayerTestSchema.
findings.fix: Reference a MODELS entry (e.g. add MODELS.standard) instead of the literal string.

findings.title: Brave Search fan-out has cold-start cache stampede (cost trivial)
findings.impact: Cache is only written after success (:154), so concurrent cold requests each fire 4 Brave API calls. Spend is bounded by the 5-min TTL and Brave's cheap pricing; unauthenticated access is acceptable for a public news ticker. Noted for completeness only.
findings.fix: Optional: in-flight promise dedupe (single-flight) around the Promise.allSettled block.

findings.title: SELECT * + spread of full users row into API response
findings.impact: Currently benign (users table has no password/secret columns — lib/db/schema.ts:8-19; OAuth-only), but any future sensitive column added to users leaks automatically to the client, and auth_provider/auth_id are already exposed.
findings.fix: Select an explicit column allowlist instead of * before launch.

findings.title: Dead import: generateSynopsis is imported but never used
findings.impact: Cosmetic — bundles an unused side-canal dependency into the route module and misleads readers into thinking this GET generates synopses (it only reads the latest stored one).
findings.fix: Delete the import line.

findings.title: Dev-only diagnostics correctly 404 in production (test-providers GET and test-key POST)
findings.impact: Both diagnostic routes are gated by NODE_ENV=production → 404 (test-providers:43, test-key/route.ts:8), so their unauthenticated app-key spends (test-providers) and key-forwarding (test-key, user-supplied keys only) are unreachable in prod. Verified working as designed — no action needed, recorded for the freeze-gate matrix.
findings.fix: None required. Optionally add the same gate pattern to any future diagnostic route.

findings.title: Anonymous updateQuery branch updates any query row by ID alone (no ownership predicate)
findings.impact: The userId==null branch runs UPDATE queries SET ... WHERE id = ? with no user_id filter. Query IDs are server-generated UUIDs so this is not injectable and only weakly guessable, but it means an unauthenticated code path can overwrite any query row if it ever obtains another user's queryId. Noted for the access-control lens; not a B3 injection issue.
findings.fix: For anonymous callers, add AND user_id IS NULL to the WHERE clause so anonymous writes can only touch anonymous rows.

findings.title: Current-tree secret hygiene is otherwise solid
findings.impact: Positive findings: (1) all env files on disk (.env, .env.bak, packages/web/.env.local, .env.local.tmp, .env.local.crypto, packages/api/.env, apps/web/.env.local) are untracked and gitignore-covered (.gitignore:130-133,56,162; packages/web/.gitignore:13-14); (2) tracked-file scan for sk-ant-/sk-or-/xai-/ghp_/whsec_/AKIA/BEGIN PRIVATE KEY/long-literal assignments hits only placeholders in docs (docs/archive/PHASE_1_TODO.md:337, packages/web/STRIPE_SETUP.md:129, etc.); (3) t3-env in lib/env.ts:9-28 correctly splits server secrets from client vars; all 19 NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars in the repo are legitimately public (Stripe publishable key + price IDs, PostHog key/host, Sentry DSN, OpenPanel client id, Reown/WalletConnect project IDs, URLs) — no secret misclassified as public; (4) no console/logger statement prints a secret env value (the key-related logs at packages/tools/src/web-search.ts:48,55 and packages/web/lib/side-canal.ts:58 only report absence); (5) next.config.js exposes no env, and the two token-prefix debug logs at packages/web/lib/db/auth.ts:157,173 are commented out.
findings.fix: None required; keep the gitleaks hook recommendation from the BLOCKER finding.

findings.title: No session rotation/revocation on login; verified: expiry enforced on the sole validate path; dev-login prod-gated
findings.impact: Positives worth recording: (1) No session-fixation risk — every login mints a fresh server-side random token and sets it, never accepting a client-supplied token. (2) validateSession (lib/db/auth.ts:156-236) is the only token-based lookup used by API routes and it enforces `expires_at > now`; grep found no other `FROM sessions WHERE token` path that skips expiry. (3) dev-login is hard-gated: returns 404 when NODE_ENV==='production' (dev-login/route.ts:13). Gap: a new login does not invalidate the user's prior sessions (no rotation/cap), so old cookies remain valid until their 30-day expiry; there is no 'log out all devices' primitive beyond per-token destroySession.
findings.fix: Consider deleting/capping prior sessions for the user on login, and add a bulk-revoke-by-user_id helper for account-compromise response. No launch blocker.

findings.title: Entire D3 posture assumes one long-lived Node process — serverless/multi-instance deploy silently breaks SSE and caches
findings.impact: connections/buffers (thought-stream.ts:181/191), queries (query-store.ts:59), event-emitter subscribers, response cache, rate-limit maps, and settingsStore are all per-process. CLAUDE.md lists vercel --prod as the deploy path: on serverless, the query POST and the SSE GET routinely land on different instances, so live thought/answer streams degrade to the DB-replay path and cache hit rates collapse. On the intended single-VPS Node server all of it works as designed. Restart data loss is acceptable everywhere except settingsStore (separate finding): rate limits reset (forgivable), caches cold-start, in-flight SSE clients reconnect and replay from SQLite.
findings.fix: Document single-process as a deployment invariant in WEBNA_STANDARDS/deploy notes, or gate vercel deploys until state is externalized.

findings.title: conversationHistory shipped to tot-consensus but never used
findings.impact: The tot branch POSTs { query, conversationHistory, queryId } to /api/tot-consensus (route.ts:315), but tot-consensus destructures conversationHistory and never references it (tot-consensus/route.ts:188) — advisors and synthesis see only the bare query. No LLM tokens wasted (dead internal HTTP payload only), but it means GTP consensus answers are context-blind in conversations, and the payload can be tens of KB for long chats.
findings.fix: Either drop conversationHistory from the internal fetch body (and from TotConsensusSchema), or actually thread a bounded slice into the synthesis prompt if context-aware consensus is wanted.

findings.title: Confirmed-good coverage: core provider plumbing timeouts all span the body read
findings.impact: For the freeze record, these are verified correct (signal spans headers AND body incl. SSE loops): all 7 callProvider paths use AbortSignal.timeout(PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS) as the fetch signal (multi-provider-api.ts:330, 439, 496, 550, 612, 670 — aborting json()/reader.read() too); synthesis-stream.ts 90s AbortController cleared only in finally (:59-60, 76); tot advisor calls keep the 35s abort armed across the json() read AND the auto-router fallback shares the same signal (tot-consensus/route.ts:107-134, openrouter.ts:68-77); react agent has an 85s abortSignal (react-agent.ts:66) and its web_search tool is independently bounded (web-search-core.ts:71 8s Brave, :116 10s DDG, 3s DDG throttle); grounding-client 8s (:61); query-pipeline CoinGecko 10s (:138); quick-query 15s (:55); dynamic-link-discovery 10s (:138); cognitive/depth llm-extractors 40s AbortController with clearTimeout in finally (cognitive/llm-extractor.ts:46-75). Note PROVIDER_TIMEOUT_MS=180s is a generous ceiling that interacts badly with 3x retry (see MED finding).
findings.fix: None — recorded as verified-good baseline for the freeze gate.

findings.title: Module-scope connections/buffers Maps require a single-process deployment
findings.impact: The registry (line 181) and buffers (line 191) are per-process memory. Under the PM2 single-process deployment implied by route.ts:614 this works; on serverless (Vercel) or PM2 cluster mode the SSE GET and query POST can land on different instances — the subscriber would receive only the synthetic 'connected' init event and no pipeline stages, silently. Worth a guard/comment before any hosting change (the Aug-8 hosting decision referenced at route.ts:615).
findings.fix: Document the single-process constraint at the top of thought-stream.ts; if multi-instance hosting is adopted, move the bus to Redis pub/sub or serve the SSE from the same request that runs the pipeline.

findings.title: Key-redaction pass mangled template placeholders (no actual leak found)
findings.impact: Values like `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=REDACTED_ANTHROPIC_KEY` and `OPENROUTER_API_KEY=REDACTED_OPENROUTER_KEYyour-key-here` (.env.example:7-8; same pattern in deploy/.env.production.template) are confusing concatenations. Verified via git history that the pre-redaction values were REPLACE_ME placeholders, not real keys — no rotation needed for these files.
findings.fix: Restore clean placeholders (sk-ant-..., sk-or-v1-...) in both templates.

findings.title: News fallback serves canned self-promotional headlines styled as news
findings.impact: Without BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY the news endpoint returns marketing lines ('Sovereign intelligence engine active', 'Grounding guard protecting against hallucination') with source 'akhai' and age 'now' (also hardcoded in NewsNotification.tsx:15-17). The payload sets fallback:true but the UI presents them in the news ticker as if they were headlines.
findings.fix: Label fallback items visibly as system status (or show nothing) instead of dressing marketing copy as news.
